issue_title: Deploying Chalice app as non- root user
issue_body:
Hi,

We have a group AWS account with a root user and several non- root users. We want the non- root users to be able to deploy Chalice functions to the group account. Trying this with a "standard" `chalice deploy` we get the following -

``` An error occurred (AccessDenied) when calling the CreateRole operation: User: 
 arn:aws:iam::261572527505:user/Ashley is not authorized to perform: 
 iam:CreateRole on resource: arn:aws:iam::261572527505:role/aws_hello_world-dev```

OK, so we figured this user didn't have sufficient permissions. We created a `Lambdas` group, added the user and gave the group `LambdaFullAccess` role, The user then changed the following in `config.json`

- manage_iam_role: false
- iam_role_arn: #{iam_role_arn}

Try again with `chalice deploy`. This time -

``` An error occurred (AccessDeniedException) when calling the ImportRestApi 
 operation: User: arn:aws:iam::261572527505:user/Ashley is not authorized to 
 perform: apigateway:POST on resource: arn:aws:apigateway:eu-west-1::/restapis```
 Ho hum. Added `AmazonAPIGatewayInvokeFullAccess` to the group permissions on the server side. No change. Tried using `manage_iam_role: true`; still not change.

At this point we figure we are just on the wrong track. Apologies if these are terrible noob questions - we like Chalice but AWS permissioning is a nightmare - but -

- are we on the right track here, modifying IAM roles on the server side ? Or do we need to make some changes to `policy-dev.json` ?
- if the former, what is the full list of roles that is required to make this work ? If the latter, what should we be adding to `policy-dev.json` ? Should we be modifying `iam_role_arn` ?

Confused :-/

So similar to #381, we have the ability to create something in boto3 but not delete it in the same way.

In boto3, we can ec2.register_image() but we have to create an ec2 *client* to call deregister_image.  This isn't very intuitive or symmetrical.

issue_title: delete_object deletes the parent directory when the object is the last element in the directory
issue_body:
I've noticed that running botocore.s3.delete_object on a key of the form `parent/file.txt` deletes both `file.txt` and `parent` when `file.txt` is the last object inside `parent`. 
Is that the expected behaviour? I would have expected to have to delete the `parent` in a separate call.

When I run the code without GPU flag, it works without any problem.
But when I run it with GPU flag, it gives me this error:

RuntimeError: Conv2D::forward_dev_impl not supported without CUDNN
```

To confirm that CUDNN installation is okay, I ran these codes on language model (https://github.com/neubig/nn4nlp2017-code/blob/master/02-lm/nn-lm-batch.py) and LSTM-sentiment modeling (https://github.com/neubig/nn4nlp2017-code/blob/master/06-rnn/sentiment-lstm.py) with GPU flag and these were executed without problem.

So I suspect probably there can be some issue on CUDNN backed convolution layer of Dynet.

FYI, I use Ubuntu 17.04 with CUDA 8.0 and CuDNN 5.1.

Thanks,
Jeongmin

Right now we don't support multiplications of tensors whose order is higher than two. An MWE:
```
W = model.add_parameters((7, 5))
X = dy.inputTensor(np.random.rand(5, 4, 3, 3), batched=True)
o = dy.parameter(W) * X
print o.dim() # ((7, 4), 3), should be ((7, 4, 3), 3)
```

issue_title: StandardScaler does not support a dataframe?
issue_body:
Given the following code:
```
df = pd.DataFrame({'A':[1,2,3,4,2,2,3,4], 'B':[4,4,4,3,3,3,7,7], 'C':[2,2,4,3,2,3,2,7]})
xd = dd.from_pandas(df, npartitions=2)
scl = StandardScaler()
scl.fit(xd)
```

I get the error
```
TypeError: 'Series' object does not support item assignment
```

Does the `dask_ml.preprocessing.StandardScaler` not support dataframes?  The documentation says it supports any of the Dask collections, so I assumed it would.  I also tried to convert it to a Dask array but that gives me a different error:  ` TypeError: 'float' object cannot be interpreted as an integer`

Maybe I'm just doing something wrong?

issue_title: deis-store-daemon incorrectly fails; Ceph clusters have more OSDs than machines
issue_body:
On initial provision, the daemons sometimes fail with error output:

```
Nov 14 13:41:44 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal systemd[1]: Starting deis-store-daemon...
Nov 14 13:42:01 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal systemd[1]: Started deis-store-daemon.
Nov 14 13:42:11 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal sh[8757]: store-daemon: creating OSD...
Nov 14 13:42:48 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal sh[8757]: store-daemon: FATAL - We have an OSD ID that isn't an integer
Nov 14 13:42:48 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal sh[8757]: store-daemon: FATAL - This likely means the monitor we tried to connect to isn't up, but others may be.
Nov 14 13:42:48 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal sh[8757]: store-daemon: FATAL - We can't proceed because we don't know if an OSD was created or not.
Nov 14 13:42:49 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal systemd[1]: deis-store-daemon.service: main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE
Nov 14 13:42:49 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal docker[9086]: Error response from daemon: No such container: deis-store-daemon
Nov 14 13:42:49 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal docker[9086]: 2014/11/14 13:42:49 Error: failed to remove one or more containers
Nov 14 13:42:49 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal systemd[1]: Unit deis-store-daemon.service entered failed state.
Nov 14 13:42:54 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal systemd[1]: deis-store-daemon.service holdoff time over, scheduling restart.
Nov 14 13:42:54 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal systemd[1]: Stopping deis-store-daemon...
Nov 14 13:42:54 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal systemd[1]: Starting deis-store-daemon...
Nov 14 13:43:04 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal systemd[1]: Started deis-store-daemon.
Nov 14 13:43:11 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal sh[9176]: store-daemon: creating OSD...
Nov 14 13:43:27 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal sh[9176]: store-daemon: created OSD 3
Nov 14 13:43:27 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal sh[9176]: store-daemon: OSD not yet initialized. Initializing...
Nov 14 13:43:29 deis-cc4a9dd1-ed85-4958-9fcd-51444b43cfb3.novalocal sh[9176]: 2014-11-14 13:43:29.708416 7f3005a6f900  0 ceph version 0.87 (c51c8f9d80fa4e0168aa52685b8de40e42758578), process ceph-osd, pid 68
<snipped...>
```

This is caused by [this check](https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/6bbccb748f604d72236e9b54c94971c1f49066f7/store/daemon/bin/boot#L25-L30) in the /bin/boot script for store-daemons. This was intended to be a more foolproof method of ensuring that OSD creation succeeds - [previously](https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/b5cb7427b3e83381afa4aa6e52c29231c5a18862/store/daemon/bin/boot#L23-L24), we had the OSDs sleep for 10 seconds after coming up to ensure the monitors were up before proceeding to create an OSD. It actually achieves this goal. However, we are actually creating more OSDs than we intend to. When we get a non-integer back from `ceph osd create`, this usually means we are looking at a monitor fault, which means that the monitor we tried to connect to isn't yet up. The create will most likely succeed anyway, as the Ceph client will connect to a different monitor to create the OSD. There doesn't seem to be a good way to trust the output from `ceph osd create`.

The result of failing this check is that the `store-daemon` container will exit. When fleet restarts it, the script will again try to create an OSD (and usually this time it will succeed), which is what results in having more OSDs than we intend.

I'm wondering if the way to handle this is rather than trying to interpret the output of `ceph osd create`, we instead run the command and then pollwait on the OSD map output until we see ourselves in the list. This will require some output cleaning which could be fragile, but I can't think of a better way to do this.

**Note**: this isn't a major issue - having more OSDs that aren't up doesn't really affect our cluster, since we require Ceph to maintain 3 copies of all data. As long as we have 3 OSDs up, our cluster should be in a `HEALTH_OK` state. But I don't know what affect this has on the [CRUSH map](http://ceph.com/docs/master/rados/operations/crush-map/), so it's a good idea for us to fix this.

issue_title: Prediction Results Do Not Match Original XGBoost
issue_body:
I trained a model with a single iteration:
```python
param = {
    'nthread': 10,
    'objective': 'multi:softprob',
    'eval_metric': 'mlogloss',
    'num_class': 3,
    'silent': 1,
    'max_depth': 5,
    'min_child_weight': 5,
    'eta': 0.5,  # learning rate
    'subsample': 1,
    'colsample_bytree': 1,
    'gamma': 0, 
    'alpha': 0,
    'lambda': 1, 
}
watchlist  = [(dtest, 'eval')]
bst = xgb.train(param, dtrain, 1, watchlist)
```

`sample` will be a numpy array with a single row of features. The output of:

```python
bst.predict(xgb.DMatrix(sample))
```

differs noticeably from the output of:
```python
bst_lite = treelite.Model.from_xgboost(bst)
bst_lite.export_lib(toolchain='gcc', libpath=model_path, verbose=True)
batch = Batch.from_npy2d(sample)
predictor = Predictor(model_path, verbose=True)
predictor.predict(batch)
```

The problem is compounded when more trees are added.

Any ideas of what could be going on here?

Thanks for your work on this @hcho3 .

issue_title: Google requiring API Target 26 in Aug/Nov 2018
issue_body:
Just wanted to put it on the devs’ radar. We’ve been limited to 19 for the last couple years, afaik, but kivy will need to support targeting 26 for new apps starting in August, and for app updates starting in November: https://android-developers.googleblog.com/2017/12/improving-app-security-and-performance.html?m=1 

If there’s currently a straightforward way to target 21 or higher then I’ll stand corrected and be very interested in hearing how.

When I was using the model to do prediction like this

> preds = model.predict(user_ids, item_ids, item_features=item_feat,user_features=user_feat, num_threads=4)
The AssertionError came out. 

>   File "/root/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/lightfm/lightfm.py", line 623, in predict
>     assert len(user_ids) == len(item_ids)
> AssertionErro

I am just wondering why should one check if len(user_ids) and len(item_ids) are equal.

issue_title: Request for Opinion: User IDs which are not 32 bit integers
issue_body:
Hi,
This is a request for advice if you have the time :).

LightFM and other libraries ask for a 32 bit integer id e.g for users. But, our user id is a UUID e.g. `0003374a-a35c-46ed-96d2-0ea32b753199`.  I was wondering what you would recommend in scenarios like these. What I have come up with is:
- Create a bidirectional dictionary either in memory or in a database to keep a UUID <-> Int mapping.  e.g. https://github.com/jab/bidict
- Use a non cryptographic hash function like MurmurHash3 or xxHash. I still have to test how bad the collisions would be for our dataset and it's impact in a real life scenario. If the collisions are minimal, maybe they could be used for recommendations?

Thanks,
Rajiv

The `master` branch implementation treats "or later" licenses as separate keys with an eventual aliases.

The `alternate-or-later-handling' branch(https://github.com/nexB/license-expression/tree/alternate-or-later-handling) implementation treats "or later" as keywords and not as separate license keys.
If this later implementation ends up a winner, if would make sense to add validation to license symbols to check if a license supports an "or later" version or not to avoid stupid things like "MIT or later"

issue_title: Need to check versions are available for current arch when creating lockfile from requirements.txt
issue_body:
### Issue description

I use `pyarrow`, and have a spec of `~=pyarrow-0.9.0` in my `requirements.txt` file. This is because the latest version on OSX is `0.9.0.post1` and the latest version on linux is `0.9.0` (neither version is available on the other architecture). No, not how I'd run my release process either...

### Expected result

I would expect that when running `pipenv install -r requirements.txt` on linux it would create a lockfile pointing to the `0.9.0` release, and on OSX it would create a lockfile pointing to the `0.9.0.post1` release.

### Actual result

It locks to `0.9.0.post1` on linux.

### Steps to replicate

```
> uname -srv
Linux 3.10.0-229.20.1.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Tue Nov 3 19:10:07 UTC 2015
> pipenv --version
pipenv, version 2018.05.18
> cat requirements.txt
pyarrow~=0.9.0
> pipenv install -r requirements.txt
Requirements file provided! Importing into Pipfile…
Pipfile.lock not found, creating…
Locking [dev-packages] dependencies…
Locking [packages] dependencies…
Updated Pipfile.lock (52f7b3)!
Installing dependencies from Pipfile.lock (52f7b3)…
An error occurred while installing pyarrow==0.9.0.post1! Will try again.
     ▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉▉ 4/4 — 00:00:02
Installing initially–failed dependencies…
Collecting pyarrow==0.9.0.post1 ▉▉▉▉▉ 0/1 — 00:00:00

  Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement pyarrow==0.9.0.post1 (from -r /tmp/pipenv-VlGKcH-requirements/pipenv-4KZHdd-requirement.txt (line 1)) (from versions: 0.2.0, 0.3.0, 0.4.0, 0.4.1, 0.5.0.post2, 0.6.0, 0.7.0, 0.7.1, 0.8.0, 0.9.0)
No matching distribution found for pyarrow==0.9.0.post1 (from -r /tmp/pipenv-VlGKcH-requirements/pipenv-4KZHdd-requirement.txt (line 1))
You are using pip version 9.0.1, however version 10.0.1 is available.
You should consider upgrading via the 'pip install --upgrade pip' command.
```

issue_title: Git dependency with specified branch in Pipfile turns into PyPI dependency in Pipfile.lock

issue_title: "ython not found" when locking dependencies in Alpine Linux
issue_body:
I'm trying to build a docker image with dependencies installed with pipenv. It fails really weirdly.

My host OS is Debian Stretch, and I'm using the Docker image `alpine:3.6`.

```
~ # python3 --version
Python 3.6.1
~ # pipenv --version
pipenv, version 11.0.2
```

This is the output of my `docker build`:

```
Step 10/14 : RUN pipenv install --dev --system --verbose
 ---> Running in d1242c945a25
Pipfile.lock (006157) out of date, updating to (9173ad)…
Locking [dev-packages] dependencies…
ython: not found
```

I can provide a minimal Pipfile and Dockerfile to reproduce the problem if needed.

issue_title: PIPENV_VENV_IN_PROJECT in .env file ignored during initial pipenv shell command
issue_body:
Put the following line into the local .env file: 
    `export PIPENV_VENV_IN_PROJECT=1`

Running `pipenv shell` creates a .venv in `~/.local...`, not the `.venv` directory as expected. 
Running `pipenv sync` then creates the local `.venv` file, but the Python interpreter is not there. 

I've done some debugging and found that when running the initial shell command, the `os.environ` commands in `environment.py` are run prior to `core.load_dot_env`. The Python globals containing the environment variables are not reloaded after the `.env` file is loaded.

Is there a reason the .env file is not loaded earlier?

issue_title: Installing django-timezone-field doesn't update lock file
issue_body:
The package "django-timezone-field" successfully installs but once it's listed as a dependency the lock file will no longer update.

Running pipenv 2018.6.25, Python 2.7.15

------------

##### Expected result

After installing django-timezone-field, the lock file should update or produce an error.

##### Actual result

It doesn't update, and any dependencies added in the future will not be added to the lock file either.

##### Steps to replicate

```sh
$ pipenv --two
$ pipenv install django-timezone-field
```

No lockfile will be created, `pipenv lock` will not create one, and future installs will not create one either.

As we know, `pip` installs packages globally, unlike `npm`. Moreover, currently `pip` does not even _try_ to deal with dependency conflicts (when you need both packages A and B, and they both depend on different versions of package C) in any way. It doesn't even notify you much about the fact it has just broken your dependencies.

Classic example, taken from [here](https://medium.com/@alonisser/things-i-wish-pip-learned-from-npm-f712fa26f5bc):
We develop our project or library and we need `splinter` and `huxley` as the dependencies. So we gonna install them:
`$ pip install splinter`
Pip installs `splinter==0.7.5` and `selenium==3.0.2` as splinter's dependency
`$ pip install huxley`

```
Collecting huxley
...
  Found existing installation: selenium 3.0.2
    Uninstalling selenium-3.0.2:
      Successfully uninstalled selenium-3.0.2
Successfully installed huxley-0.5 selenium-2.35.0
```

So we likely broke `splinter`, and, moreover, Pip said that everything was successfull. It even trolled us by saying `successfully uninstalled package-that-we-use`. And the user probably didn't notice anything, we don't read the logs line by line, when script ended successfully. It is bad.

Is it planned to define within this spec a behavior different from described above, when the packages with incompatible dependencies will be brought together in single `Pipfile`?

issue_title: ResourceManager for Non-Package Data Files is not working as described in the documentation
issue_body:
I want to install a data file which is in my repository root when installing my package. I don't want to install it in a specific directory. It's a list of contributors which is loaded by an about dialog. So this file is not a package data file.

My setup.py:

    setup(
        ...,
        packages = find_packages('src'),
        data_files = [
            ('share/myproject', ['CONTRIBUTORS.txt'])
        ])

The file is installed in ``$prefix/share/myproject``. The actual path is irrelevant for me.

The documentation tells me:

    The data files can also be accessed using the ResourceManager API, 
    by specifying a Requirement instead of a package name

    from pkg_resources import Requirement, resource_filename
    filename = resource_filename(Requirement.parse("myproject"),"share/myproject/CONTRIBUTORS.txt")

This returns a path relative to the ``site-packages`` directory. When installed in developer mode with ``pip install --user -e .`` this is relative to the ``src`` directory (from the ``find_packages`` call?). Both paths are not pointing to the correct file as they should if the documentation is correct.

If the ``data_file`` option is a supported feature then either the documentation is wrong or the ResourceManager is using the wrong path. If this is not supported any more then it needs to be removed from the documentation.

issue_title: 36.2.1 appears to ignore install_requires with extras
issue_body:
Travis, Appveyor, and local builds of magic-wormhole started failing about an hour ago, when 36.2.1 was released. Our setup.py has:

```python
      install_requires=[
          "spake2==0.7", "pynacl",
          "six",
          "twisted[tls] >= 17.5.0", # 17.5.0 adds failAfterFailures=
          "autobahn[twisted] >= 0.14.1",
          "automat",
          "hkdf",
          "tqdm >= 4.13.0", # 4.13.0 fixes crash on NetBSD
          "click",
          "humanize",
          "ipaddress",
          "txtorcon >= 0.19.3",
      ],
```

When I run `pip install -e .` in a virtualenv with setuptools-36.2.0, I see Autobahn-17.7.1 installed as expected. If I have setuptools-36.2.1, then Autobahn does *not* get installed. (Twisted *does* get installed, but it's a dependency of txtorcon).

```
$ curl -i https://virtualenv.pypa.io/
HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
Content-Type: text/html
Fastly-Restarts: 1
Transfer-Encoding: chunked
Accept-Ranges: bytes
Date: Mon, 11 Jul 2016 11:05:51 GMT
Via: 1.1 varnish
Connection: keep-alive
X-Served-By: cache-lhr6331-LHR
X-Cache: MISS
X-Cache-Hits: 0
Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31557600; includeSubDomains

curl: (18) transfer closed with outstanding read data remaining
```

Module names which python differentiates by case - ie DNS vs dns are not handled correctly on case-insensitive operating systems(like OSX) by default.  The contents of the modules are packaged into the same directory and the files can potentially conflict.  

For a simple example, the dnspython & py3dns are incompatible out of the box on OSX, while they do not conflict as system packages.

After merging PR #63 links written in bold aren't supported anymore. Example input: ``` **[A bold link](http://example.com)** ``` Results in an uncaught exception: ``` Error Traceback (most recent call last): File "/usr/lib/python3.5/unittest/case.py", line 59, in testPartExecutor yield File "/usr/lib/python3.5/unittest/case.py", line 597, in run self.setUp() File "/home/projects/CMS/recommonmark/tests/test_sphinx.py", line 50, in setUp self.app.build(force_all=True) File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/sphinx/application.py", line 327, in build self.builder.build_all() File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/sphinx/builders/__init__.py", line 211, in build_all self.build(None, summary='all source files', method='all') File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/sphinx/builders/__init__.py", line 265, in build self.doctreedir, self.app)) File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/sphinx/environment/__init__.py", line 556, in update self._read_serial(docnames, app) File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/sphinx/environment/__init__.py", line 576, in _read_serial self.read_doc(docname, app) File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/sphinx/environment/__init__.py", line 684, in read_doc pub.publish() File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/docutils/core.py", line 217, in publish self.settings) File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/sphinx/io.py", line 55, in read self.parse() File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/docutils/readers/__init__.py", line 78, in parse self.parser.parse(self.input, document) File "/home/projects/CMS/recommonmark/recommonmark/parser.py", line 36, in parse self.convert_ast(ast) File "/home/projects/CMS/recommonmark/recommonmark/parser.py", line 47, in convert_ast fn(node) File "/home/projects/CMS/recommonmark/recommonmark/parser.py", line 134, in visit_link else mdnode.parent.sourcepos[0][0]) TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable ``` Adding a space between `**` and the link won't cause an error, but the output is not shown as expected. Example input: ``` ** [A bold link](http://example.com) ** ``` Output (HTML): ```

** A bold link **

```

issue_title: I Need Help Using Popovers with Bootstrap 2 (bootstrap-sass 2.3.2.2)
issue_body:
I'm attempting popovers with bootstrap-sass for the first time in a Rails 4.2.5 application but without any success. 

Here is my setup in my Rails application.

CSS

**app/assets/stylesheets/custom.css.scss**

```
@import "bootstrap";

.popover { max-width: none; }
```
JS

**app/assets/javascripts/bootstrap.js.coffee**

```
jQuery ->
  $(".popover-map").popover({ html : true })
  # $("a[rel~=popover], .has-popover").popover()
```

**app/assets/javascripts/application.js** 

`//= require bootstrap`

**Rails partial code**

`<%= link_to("#{t :map_marfa_head}" , '#', class: "popover-map", rel: "popover", title: "#{t :map_marfa_head}", :"data-placement" => "bottom", :"data-content" => "#{render 'pages/map_marfa'}") %>`

This code worked when I was using the twitter-bootstrap-rails gem. The only difference was the @import statement in my stylesheet and the require statement in application.js. When using bootstrap-sass I get the following error when I view the page source.

```
TypeError: $(".popover-map").popover is not a function. (In '$(".popover-map").popover({
      html: true
    })', '$(".popover-map").popover' is undefined)
(anonymous function)bootstrap.self-a54a326f6cd027d08c61488151646f3b32c14ca968788b351d38b24c02b72000.js:2
firejquery.self-c64a74367bda6ef8b860f19e74df08927ca99d2be2ac934e9e92d5fd361e0da4.js:3232
fireWithjquery.self-c64a74367bda6ef8b860f19e74df08927ca99d2be2ac934e9e92d5fd361e0da4.js:3362
readyjquery.self-c64a74367bda6ef8b860f19e74df08927ca99d2be2ac934e9e92d5fd361e0da4.js:3582
completedjquery.self-c64a74367bda6ef8b860f19e74df08927ca99d2be2ac934e9e92d5fd361e0da4.js:3617
```
I have tried commenting out the statement that got the error and uncommenting the other statement in **app/assets/javascripts/bootstrap.js.coffee**. I still get the error that popover is not a function.

I also tried to add each of the following statements in **app/assets/javascripts/application.js** (one at a time).

`//= require bootstrap/bootstrap-popover`
`//= require bootstrap/bootstrap-popover.js`

For both require statements I get the following additional error.

```
TypeError: undefined is not an object (evaluating '$.fn.tooltip.Constructor')
(anonymous function)bootstrap-popover.self-f7ccec381e00cb3f9bde10f269cac0145d4926fa87aa0e0a184c5629a7c1d58d.js:37
(anonymous function)bootstrap-popover.self-f7ccec381e00cb3f9bde10f269cac0145d4926fa87aa0e0a184c5629a7c1d58d.js:114
```

From what I read on the main GitHub page the entire package is installed by default. I assume that means all I need to correct my setup somehow to use popovers. I'm not finding much online regarding this error. On GitHub I cannot find any documentation regarding versions of bootstrap-sass for Bootstrap 2. However I'm not sure if the version of Bootstrap is the issue.

UPDATE 3/2/2016 9:45 am CST: I decided to move my tooltip statement to be the first line. I get the same error for tooltip as well when I view the page source. It appears that the version of the gem I'm using does not include tooltip or popover like the twitter-bootstrap-rails does. I'm not sure what to do at this point since I'm new to using this gem.

Any help would be appreciated.

issue_title: [Batch] Command returns parse error for resource not found
issue_body:
```
$ az batch application show -g dfgdfg -n dfgfhg --application-id dffdggfh 
usage: az batch application show [-h] [--output {json,tsv,table,jsonc}]
                                 [--verbose] [--debug] [--query JMESPATH]
                                 --resource-group RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME
                                 --application-id APPLICATION_ID --name
                                 ACCOUNT_NAME
az batch application show: error: Azure Error: ResourceGroupNotFound
Message: Resource group 'dfgdfg' could not be found.
```

Specifying a resource group that isn't found is not a parsing error.
A resource exists check should not happen here.

```
def application_enabled(namespace):
    """Validates account has auto-storage enabled"""
    from azure.mgmt.batch import BatchManagementClient
    from azure.cli.core.commands.client_factory import get_mgmt_service_client

    client = get_mgmt_service_client(BatchManagementClient)
    acc = client.batch_account.get(namespace.resource_group_name, namespace.account_name)
    if not acc:
        raise ValueError("Batch account '{}' not found.".format(namespace.account_name))
    if not acc.auto_storage or not acc.auto_storage.storage_account_id:  # pylint: disable=no-member
        raise ValueError("Batch account '{}' needs auto-storage enabled.".
                         format(namespace.account_name))
```
For `batch application` commands, there's a validator called `application_enabled` causing a parse error.

This is inconsistent with the rest of the CLI.

We can onboard you to `microsoft/azure-cli` image with a tag like `:python-preview`. 

Also please install stuff like `jq` to the docker image, you're nearly always returning json responses, it's impossible to read. All these was solved in the `microsoft/azure-cli` docker image, feel free to refer to its Dockerfile for reference.

issue_title: [DNS] zone import should strip trailing .@ from relative record names
issue_body:
The following is a legitimate DNS record from a BIND zone file:

_sip._tls.@	3600 	IN	SRV	100	1	443	sipdir.online.lync.com

Note the '.@' in the record name, which currently causes import to fail (Bad Request).

Where a record name ends in .@, we should strip it off (and then apply the current value of $ORIGIN).

issue_title: [WebApp] Delete command should be more transparent about deleting plans
issue_body:
### Description

If you call the `az webapp delete` command, and the specified webapp instance is the only one within the App Service plan, then it will delete the plan as well. Personally, I found this behavior pretty confusing (since you have no way of knowing it's happening), but more importantly, it wasn't what I wanted at all, so I had to re-create the plan again.

The portal makes this behavior pretty clear when you're attempting to delete a web app instance, and it also allows you to prevent deleting the plan. It would be great if the CLI allowed suppressing the plan deletion behavior (some new flag?) and/or maybe it would make sense to display a prompt that notifies the user that they're about to delete the only web app, and ask whether they'd like to also delete the plan. issue_title: Verify LRO PATCH as per our discussion
issue_body: Long running PATCH operation with initial status code as 200 and with azure-asyncoperation or location header in the response should be supported by the runtime.

Currently when running `application upload`, on every request made a user is prompted for authentication when connecting to secure clusters. This also slows down uploads.

Instead, a single session should be used and multiple requests sent through the session.

### Description:
Hi there,

If 2.6 supported? The thing is CentOS features this one python version out-of-box. 
We make an installable software for Linux. We use it to communicate with azure prior installing anything on the server. 

At the moment, a run of our code produces whenever we do 'import azure.storage':
File "/usr/lib/python2.6/site-packages/azure/storage/__init__.py", line 1197
     name.lower() : value for name, value in request.headers if value
SyntaxError: invalid syntax

Is there any means to fix that one incompatibility?
Thanks,
Vlad

##### Original Issue: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/issues/395
### Comments (1):
#### @crwilcox commented on 2015-06-24 00:18:12:
> The issue is that Python 2.6 does not support dictionary comprehensions.

We don't officially support 2.6 and don't make changes to address issues in 2.6.  That being said, this one is rather simple.  I would have little issue taking a pull request that addresses this

The way to fix this is to remove the dict comp and replace it with dict().
 

```python
{
    name.lower() : value for name, value in request.headers if value
}
```

to 

```python
dict((name.lower(), value) for name, value in request.headers if value)
```

Again, you are welcome to patch this on your own or submit a pull request for us to take.

Thanks!

Make sure the value of Authorization header is formed correctly including the signature. AuthenticationFailedServer failed to authenticate the request. like 
```
   env:
       - KERAS_BACKEND=theano
```

xref: https://github.com/conda-forge/keras-feedstock/pull/6

issue_title: Ability to "conda env create" from Anaconda cloud with --no-deps argument?
issue_body:
I've created a an environment that I want to use on https://anaconda.org/ajoros/ves_env_win64
[pardon my newbieness] 

Is it possible to perform a command like:

`conda env create ajoros/ves_env_win64`

without it installing the dependencies of the packages in my environment.yaml. I am looking for `conda env create` to mimic the action that occurs when using `conda install --no-deps <package name>`. 

For example it installs` matplotlib=1.5.1=np111py35_0`, but it also installs all its dependencies like pyqt and qt when I dont want it to. I want it to install the base matplotlib package without any dependencies. I want to do this because later on I install @mmcauliffe's version of pyqt5 and qt5 from anaconda cloud (as you can see in the yaml file on Anaconda Cloud)

In short, I'm looking to run `conda env create ajoros/ves_env_win64` and create an environment of **ONLY** the packages listed in my yaml file, and nothing more ... (See YML file here:  http://pastebin.com/MXcnpdqh)

Thanks

aj

Saw this in a recent build log. Based on consensus, we thought it was coming from `conda`. That said, this is a `conda-build` key so....

In general would figure other build tools using `conda` could register keys they use from the config, but IDK if that is a thing we can do with `conda` currently. Also avoiding conflicts can get tricky.

Perhaps for now it is ok if `conda` learns the `conda-build` key is ok (even if it is a poor separation of concerns). Long term some sort of config discussion would be good.

ref: https://circleci.com/gh/conda-forge/conda-smithy-feedstock/220

cc @isuruf @msarahan

issue_title: Polynomial coefficients cause truncation errors for integer data arrays
issue_body:
The following currently fails:
```python
data = [10, 29, 33]
intarray = self.block.create_data_array("intarray", "array",
                                        nix.DataType.Int64,
                                        data=data)
intarray.polynom_coefficients = (0.0, 0.1)
np.testing.assert_almost_equal(intarray[:], np.array(data) * 0.1)
```

The data read from `intarray` gets converted to `[1, 2, 3]` because the array's dtype constrains the return value to Int64.

This doesn't happen in NIX.

The following is valid Seneca code and will cause an infinite loop without an explicit definition of one.
```
a = []
b = []

a.append(b)
b.append(a)

def uh_oh(x):
    uh_oh(x[0])

uh_oh(a)
```

issue_title: Pulp should support more conservative dependency solving
issue_body:
### Description

Copying rpm units between repositories should only associate units that the target repository misses to satisfy any dependencies:

- [ ]  create a `zoo` repository and populate it with the [Zoo repository content](https://repos.fedorapeople.org/repos/pulp/pulp/demo_repos/zoo/)
- [ ] create an empty repository `foo`
- [ ] copy the `penguin` from the `zoo` repository to the `foo` repository
- [ ] copy the `zebra` unit from the `zoo` repository to the `foo` repository
- [ ] check that just the unsatisfied `zebra` dependencies were pulled in
- [ ] check that both the `penguin` and `zebra` units have their dependencies satisfied in the `foo` repository

### Notes
🐞 https://pulp.plan.io/issues/2478

https://pulp.plan.io/issues/2274
pulp-smash issue #490

Check if the error message is sane when upload a duplicated ISO file.

This is a qpid bug

Messages in resource manager queue are not persisted after restarting Qpid and cause tasks never start

1. systemctl stop pulp_resource_manager
2. Sync a repo
3. systemctl restart qpidd
4. systemctl start pulp_resource_manager

issue_title: Verify Scheduled RPM installation and uninstallation work
issue_body:
[Issue 2680](https://pulp.plan.io/issues/2680) tracks an issue where scheduling RPM installation and uninstallation does not work. To verify this fix do the following:

1. Set up a blank Pulp 2.12 environment with at least one consumer (this assumes the consumer is called `myconsumer`).

2. Create an RPM repository (I used the Fedora "zoo" demo repo). This was done with:

`pulp-admin rpm repo create --repo-id=zoo --relative-url=zoo --feed=http://repos.fedorapeople.org/repos/pulp/pulp/demo_repos/zoo/`.

3. Sync and publish that repo with:

`pulp-admin rpm repo sync run --repo-id zoo`
`pulp-admin rpm repo publish run --repo-id zoo`

4. Bind it to the consumer.  `pulp-admin rpm consumer bind --repo-id zoo --consumer-id myconsumer`

5. Schedule the installation of an RPM package. The command below was used, but you need to adjust the date so it targets a point in time that's just a few minutes in the future

`pulp-admin rpm consumer package install schedules create --schedule R1/2017-03-29T14:20:00Z/PT1H --consumer-id myconsumer --name tiger`

6. Wait until the schedule triggers

7. Check the results

issue_title: As a User I would like to sync SUSE errata
issue_body:
https://pulp.plan.io/issues/3377
## Testing with actual SUSE repo:
1. Register for SUSE developer subscription at  https://www.suse.com/subscriptions/sles/developer/
2. Get SUSE repo info using the developer subscrption token with jmitsch's script: https://gist.github.com/johnpmitsch/0297e7b0c6ac7e261dbfd559d4903133
3. Get the token for https://updates.suse.com/SUSE/Updates/SLE-SERVER/12-SP3/x86_64/update/
4. Create a repo: `pulp-admin rpm repo create --repo-id suse-updates --feed=https://updates.suse.com/SUSE/Updates/SLE-SERVER/12-SP3/x86_64/update/`
5. update token on the repo ` http --auth admin:admin --json PUT https://pulp2/pulp/api/v2/repositories/suse-updates/ importer_config:='{"query_auth_token": "YOURTOKEN"}'`
6. sync the repo: `pulp-admin rpm repo sync run --repo-id=suse-updates`

Things to look for in the published `/var/lib/pulp/published/..../repodata/updateinfo.xml`:
- A package with `restart_suggested` 
- A package with `relogin_suggested`

## Testing with Fixture
To simplify these test I would recommend creating a suse errata fixture. Here is an sample SUSE updateinfo.xml: https://paste.fedoraproject.org/paste/bV5Gom3Jpu0hihF535VNpQ

It has the following differences from a yum/dnf updateinfo.xml:

- No `<updated date=""/>` timestamp for updated Errata
- Different datetime format for `<issued date="">` timestamp
- `<update version="">` is incremented for updated Errata
- some packages have a `restart_suggested` field
- some packages have a `relogin_suggested` field

issue_title: Issue with classes inheriting from Resource
issue_body:
When creating a class that inherits from Resource, any resource generated from inside the subclass is initialized as the subclass (not as Resource).

Exemple:

```
from rdflib import *
from rdflib.resource import Resource
from rdflib.namespace import RDF, FOAF

class Person( Resource ):

	def __init__(self, graph, subject):
		super(Person, self).__init__(graph, subject)
		self.add( RDF.type, FOAF.Person )

	def interest(self):
		return self.value( FOAF.interest ) # that's the point
		
g = Graph()
mike = Person( g, URIRef('http://example.com/mike' ) )
any = Resource( g, URIRef('http://example.com/any' ) )
mike.add( FOAF.interest, any)
interest = mike.interest() 
print g.serialize(format='turtle')
```
Outputs:
```
...
@prefix ns1: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/> .
<http://example.com/mike> a ns1:Person ;
    ns1:interest <http://example.com/any> .
<http://example.com/any> a ns1:Person .     <---- Who said that!?
```


Cheers,
Marc

issue_title: remove "update_metric" in module faces weird performance
issue_body:
Hi,

I faced an issue involves "update_metric" in module(https://github.com/dmlc/mxnet/blob/master/python/mxnet/module/base_module.py#L875). 
Based on the document, this function is to 
"Evaluate and accumulate evaluation metric on outputs of the last forward computation" e.g. train accuracy.

The problem is that the end-user creates with the help of CMake the xcode project file , opens it and builds the executable but when he tries to run it he get's a runtime error which has mypath where i built assimp (e.g. Users/..../assimp3-1-1-build/) reference to libassimpd.3.dylib "Image not found" .

issue_title: Refactoring Android logcat to allow for publishing and subscribing
issue_body:
The [mobly.controller.android_device](https://github.com/google/mobly/blob/master/mobly/controllers/android_device.py) logcat functionality currently creates a file in `/tmp/logs` ([populated by the Mobly test runner](https://github.com/google/mobly/blob/master/mobly/logger.py#L135)).

While this allows for test artifact preservation, it doesn't allow the test to easily make use of the live logcat stream. A test could attach a Unix `tail -f` subprocess but due to an [issue](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/linux/+bug/882147) with `inotify` on OverlayFS on Linux, it is often the case that any particular logcat line may be delayed and, therefore, cannot be used for realtime processing.

Another approach would be to create an entirely new logcat publisher class (a subclass of `threading.Thread`) and subscriber with `subscribe()` and `unsubscribe()` methods along with a logcat subscriber class (an abstract base class) that would handle new log lines. An example of a subscriber class would something that simply saves all raw logcat lines to a file (such as the current system) or something that's monitoring a particular regular expression and updating some state variables.

issue_title: Is there a way to create a custom dictionary?
issue_body:
Most files of our project have data that is not text, but delta-encoded floats and shorts, and I think it would greatly benefit from creating a dictionary with the most common strings.

Related #165

I was running fuzz testing on [my Brotli implementation](https://github.com/dsnet/compress/tree/master/brotli) and noticed that the following input differs when decompressed by my version compared to the canonical one here.

The input causing issues (in hex): `e2e184886a563080e0391603e430f9`

When decompressed by the `google/brotli` C implementation: 
`worldwhitemediafirstirstirstirstirstirstirstirstirstirstirst...`
When decompressed by my implementation: 
`worldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorldorl...`

According to section 4 of the RFC:
>   When a distance symbol 0 appears, the distance it represents (i.e.,
   the last distance in the sequence of distances) is not pushed to the
   ring buffer of last distances

However, this seems contrary to [what C decoder currently does](https://github.com/google/brotli/blob/ccabf811ffcef087d9f10cf417b05271c2067a82/dec/decode.c#L1706-L1710):
```c
if (s->distance_code >= 0) {
	--s->dist_rb_idx;
	s->distance_code = s->dist_rb[s->dist_rb_idx & 3];
	goto postReadDistance;  /* We already have the implicit distance */
}
```

The logic in the C version seems contrary to the stated prose in the RFC, which makes no mention that an implicit zero *reverse* rotates the distance ring-buffer.

issue_title: Support case-insensitive usage of Python Fire CLIs
issue_body:
Much like how we allow users to use hyphens `-` in place of underscores, this Issue is to allow users to use all-lowercase versions of identifiers.

**Typical case**
```
import fire
class CaseExample(object):
  def Alpha():
    return 'path 1'
fire.Fire(CaseExample)
```

```
$ python example.py Alpha  # This is the current behavior.
path 1
$ python example.py alpha  # This is the new behavior I want to add.
path 1
$ python example.py ALPHA   # Do we want to support other capitalizations? Or just the all lower-case version?
path 1
```

**Corner cases**
Unlike with the -/_ support, there can potentially be members with multiple capitalization variants of the name.

E.g.
```
import fire
class CaseExample(object):
  def alpha():
    return 'path 1'
  def Alpha():
    return 'path 2'

fire.Fire(CaseExample)
```

If the user provides an exact match, it's clear what we should do. I'm open to discussion about how to handle the ambiguous case.

```
$ python example.py alpha
path 1
$ python example.py Alpha
path 2
$ python example.py ALPHA
WARNING: Ambiguous member access `ALPHA`; accessing `alpha`.
path 1
```

**Why?**
The primary motivation is that command line tools typically use lower case commands, whereas Python classes (and occasionally methods/functions) typically have capital names. By allowing these capital names to be used via their lower-case variant, the CLIs that result from normal python code will feel natural more often.

**Possible extension**
If we also support dropping/adding -/_s in some situations, then people could use snake-case to refer to CamelCase items... just brainstorming. Thoughts?

issue_title: non-printable characters are not supported inside quoted scalars
issue_body:
YAML standard [section 5.1](http://www.yaml.org/spec/1.2/spec.html#id2770814) explicitly allows non-printables in quoted scalars for JSON compatibility, `js-yaml` doesn't:

```
> require('js-yaml').load('"\ud800"')
JS-YAML: the stream contains non-printable characters at line 1, column 1:
    "���"
    ^
```

```
> JSON.parse('"\ud800"')
'���'
```

issue_title: Rename sos execute to sos task?
issue_body:
Interface wise, we currently have

```
sos execute task_id
sos execute task_id --query
sos execute task_id --kill
```

but 

```
sos task execute task_id
sos task query task_id
sos task kill task_id
```

might read better.

We coud have

```
sos execute task_id
sos query task_id
sos kill task_id
```

but not sure if we should devote three (or more) subcommands for external tasks.

Note that `query` and `kill` are mostly for internal use (called by `run`) but users could use them from command line and there can be addons that use these tools, for example, to provide GUI for job monitoring.

issue_title: "unknown compression record" for some NEXRAD files
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm getting "unknown compression record" errors when reading some NEXRAD level 2 archive files, for example [KDIX19960521_185016.gz](https://noaa-nexrad-level2.s3.amazonaws.com/1996/05/21/KDIX/KDIX19960521_185016.gz). Here is the code to reproduce the bug:

    import pyart
    radar = pyart.io.read('KDIX19960521_185016.gz')

I have traced the problem down to the elif statement in [io/nexrad_level2.py](https://github.com/ARM-DOE/pyart/blob/master/pyart/io/nexrad_level2.py#L141). The compression record for some NEXRAD level 2 files is `b'\t\x80'` instead of `b'\x00\x00'`. Other than the unexpected compression record entry, KDIX19960521_185016.gz seems to be a valid radar scan. A solution that worked for me was to replace the elif statement in [io/nexrad_level2.py](https://github.com/ARM-DOE/pyart/blob/master/pyart/io/nexrad_level2.py#L141) with the following:

    elif compression_record[compression_slice] in {b'\x00\x00', b'\t\x80'}:

issue_title: null column values in styling not handled correctly
issue_body:
Dataset is earthquakes and styling for both color and size is ramp of magnitude (5 bins, equal interval). Null values in the column used for styling are being colored as though they are in the highest color bin using CARTOframes.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12551177/32517488-9956e03a-c3d4-11e7-9786-9e4b60c096e1.png)

Equivalent styling in CARTO Builder shows that the null values default to the lowest color in the ramp.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12551177/32517628-0152530e-c3d5-11e7-91ea-d70f1340be9f.png)

We should change this so the null values correspond to the lowest value of ramp, the same as in Builder.

Would be great to implement the initialization scheme proposed in https://arxiv.org/abs/1707.02937 and recommend it in the `TransposedConv2DLayer`. Could be a "meta-initializer" that can be used to wrap any of the existing initializers (wrapping `GlorotUniform` by default). Should tackle #858 on the way (i.e., ensure the gain is chosen correctly -- I think this would come for free).

The implementation would be something like:
```python
class ICNR(Initializer):
    def __init__(self, stride, initializer=GlorotUniform()):
        self.stride = stride
        self.initializer = initializer
    def sample(self, shape):
        stride = as_tuple(self.stride, len(shape) - 2)
        subshape = shape[:2] + tuple(l // s for l, s in zip(shape[2:], stride))
        result = self.initializer(subshape)
        for d, s in enumerate(stride):
            result = np.repeat(result, s, axis=2 + d)
        return result[(slice(None), slice(None)) + tuple(slice(None, l) for l in shape[2:])]
```

The name is taken from the paper, a less cryptic one would be preferred.

issue_title: parse_datetime wrong accepts datetimes that mix extended and basic format
issue_body:
This isn't legal under ISO 8601 and should throw an error:

```
>>> ciso8601.parse_datetime('20010203T04:05:06Z')
datetime.datetime(2001, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)
```

So should this:

```
>>> ciso8601.parse_datetime('20010203T04:05')
datetime.datetime(2001, 2, 3, 4, 5)
```

The first is forbidden by the combination of section 4.3.2, which specifies the format for "complete" date and time representations, and section 4.4.4, about date *interval* representations. Section 4.3.2 says:

> **The  hyphen  [-] and  the  colon  [:]  shall  be  used, in accordance with 4.4.4**, as separators within the date and time of day expressions, respectively, when required.

And section 4.4.4.1, says:

> When the application identifies the need for a complete representation of a time interval, identified by its start and its end, it  shall  use an expression in accordance with  4.4.2  combining any  two  complete date and time of day representations as defined in 4.3.2, **provided that the resulting expression is either consistently in basic format or consistently in extended format**.

It's kind of weird that the section about how to represent complete datetimes basically says "Hey, to know all the rules about how to do this, go forward in the spec and read the unrelated section about datetime intervals, because we actually put some of our rules about what a legal datetime representation in *there* for some reason", but as far as I can tell that's exactly what section 4.3.2 says. It would've been more natural to put *all* the rules about what a legal complete datetime representation is in the section about complete datetime representations and refer back to *that* from the section about intervals, but that's not what the authors did. Meh.

The second example I gave above, with a "reduced accuracy" datetime, is more straightforward; it's banned by section 4.3.3, which says

> For reduced accuracy, decimal or expanded representations of date and time of day, any of the
representations in *[bla]* followed immediately by the time designator [T] may be combined with any of the representations in *[bla]* provided that
>
> a) *[bla]*;
> b) *[bla]*;
> c) *[bla]*;
> d) **the expression shall either be completely in basic format**, in which case the minimum number of separators necessary for the required expression is used, **or completely in extended format**, in which case additional separators shall be used in accordance with 4.1 and 4.2.

issue_title: Edit mode not preserving things properly
issue_body:
Entering and then immediately leaving edit mode on a worksheet doesn't seem to preserve formatting.

Example:
```
$ test_cmd=$(cat <<EOF
> echo hello
> echo hello2
> EOF
> )

$ cl run -n test_break --- "$test_cmd"
0xad88a05b2d1141338425ef6b6b2c6060

$ cl cat ^1
name    perm  size
------------------
stderr  0644     0
stdout  0644    13

$ cl cat ^1/stdout
hello
hello2
```
Notice that the `test_cmd` will contain a newline since there was no backslash at the end of the `echo hello` line.

Even with this extra newline, the bundle looks ok in the worksheet.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2111202/24822710/2273f294-1bac-11e7-9c07-c5e04829b535.png)

However, if you go into edit mode (even if you don't change anything) and then save out of it, the formatting of the bundle breaks:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2111202/24822737/6ba9b66a-1bac-11e7-99bb-08371f6aee18.png)

Presumably because it now "sees" the extraneous newline.

issue_title: Incorrect solution for a simple linear programming problem.
issue_body:
The code below solves the linear programming problem:
min -x
s.t. -2x <= 0
This problem is evidently unbounded, but the pulp solver gives out a status of 1, indicating a bounded solution.

```python
from pulp import *

prob = LpProblem('test', LpMinimize)
x = LpVariable('x')

prob += -1 * x
prob += -2 * x <= 0

print(prob.solve())
```

issue_title: Error Passing Session Configuration for presto using SQLAlchemy
issue_body:
When passing session configuration using `connect_args` in SQLAlchemy as documented in README,

`create_engine('presto://user@host:443/hive', connect_args={'query_max_run_time': '1234m'})`

it throws the following error:
`StatementError: (builtins.TypeError) __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'query_max_run_time' [SQL: 'select * from my_table limit 10']`

This can be fixed by passing the connect_args as follows:
`create_engine('presto://user@host:443/hive', connect_args={'session_props':{'query_max_run_time': '1234m'}})`

Created a pull request updating this.

issue_title: Fallback language not working with multiple fields
issue_body:
I might be doing something wrong here but it looks like the fallback doesn't work when the translation exists for a different field.
I have a model like:
```
class MyModel(TranslatableModel):
    title = TranslatedField(any_language=True)
    description = TranslatedField(any_language=True)
    translations = TranslatedFields(
        title=models.CharField(max_length=255),
        description=models.TextField(blank=True),
    )
```

If I create an object with a translation for both fields in 'en', then add a translated description for 'de', when I retrieve that object in 'de' I would expect to have the German description and the English title (as a fallback). What happens instead is that, since the German translation exists, I get the German description and an empty title.
I took a quick look at the tests and it appears this case is not covered, since they all seem to deal with a single "title" field. Of course, maybe I'm just doing something wrong, in which case please let me know.

issue_title: OPTIONS Request in Flask uses content-type of None
Using a GET request with a request body.
issue_body:
It appears that the elastic search client uses a GET request with a body for the query DSL endpoint. I just discovered an issue with Google App Engine's HTTP fetching were the GET body is stripped. To my knowledge, the HTTP standard does not specify what should happen with a the body of a GET request provided it does not change the semantics of the request. The GET method makes sense since the query is an idempotent, (mostly) cache-able read request. 

Perhaps it would be better funnel the query DSL json through an alternative method or a parameter on the GET request? This would reduce the possibility of intermediary proxies doing strange things.

I filed a bug with Google to have them fix it on their end, but the client should not use non-standard functionality.

Issue with Google: https://code.google.com/p/googleappengine/issues/detail?id=13571

issue_title: Support observe as a decorator outside of classes
issue_body:
Please note that I have only used `traitlets` as a part of `ipywidgets`, and not sure how feasible are the suggestions below for its other uses - but for widgets they would be quite useful.

So, when one needs to `observe` a widget trait, it can't be done without separately defining the handler function and then subscribing:

```python
def handler(*_):
    # ... code ...

w.observe(handler, 'value')
```

Wouldn't it be better if you could just write like this:

```python
@w.observe('value')
def _(*_):
    # ... code ...
```

This way there is also no need to make a name for the handle function.

However, it's often useful to call the handler once after setting everything up, e.g. if you make some plots in the handler it makes sense to display them without forcing user to move a slider or something. So, I also suggest adding an argument to `observe`, like `run=True` (looking for a better name :) ) or a separate method `observe_and_run` which would also execute the handler (in addition to subscribing). This way we still wouldn't have to think of a name for the handler function.

So, instead of:

```python
def handler(*_):
    # ... code ...

w.observe(handler, 'value')
handler()
```

we would be able to write:

```python
@w.observe('value', run=True)
def _(*_):
    # ... code ...
```

issue_title: nbgrader doesn't work when jupyterhub has SSL enabled.
issue_body:
We're having a problem with getting formgrade to work with jupyterhub when jupyterhub has SSL enabled.  When I turn SSL on in jupyterhub by specifying an ssl_key and ssl_cert, jupyterhub is fine, but the nbgrader formgrade application becomes inaccessible.  I am not sure what is going on, but nothing shows up in the formgrade logs, and I'm not sure where to find logging from the proxy server.  What you see in the browser when you hit 'https://<domain>/hub/nbgrader/<classname>" is ongoing loading, like what you see when you visit a page using HTTP that is only available in HTTPS.  It doesn't time out quick, it doesn't get an error, it just keeps spinning, and it can go for minutes.

I think this is similar to the problem outlined in issue 178 ( [https://github.com/jupyter/nbgrader/pull/178](https://github.com/jupyter/nbgrader/pull/178) ) - there, in file nbgrader/auth/hubauth.py, the domain needed to be configurable in the auth URL.  I think we also need to be able to configure the protocol string at the beginning, so it can be "https".

In addition, I expect we'll also want this to carry through to the rendering of the URLs inside the formgrade application pages, as well, so they also use the same protocol string.

But if you look at the [test code](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/master/notebook/tests/notebook/save.js#L75), you see that there are two more asserts following the ``checkpoints OK`` one. To confirm, if you add ``this.test.assert(false);`` in the last ``then``, you'll still get a passing test.

I tried debugging by adding some logging at the beginning of the ``notebook/save.js`` file :

    casper.on('remote.message', function(message) {

    casper.options.onStepComplete = function(casper, result) {

`File "createRepo.py", line 72, in remote.push(['refs/heads/master']) File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pygit2/remote.py", line 470, in push check_error(err) File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pygit2/errors.py", line 64, in check_error raise GitError(message) _pygit2.GitError: authentication required but no callback set`


    ```
    #Clone the newly created repo

    for file in files:
        os.rename("/var/www/html/uploads/" + file, "/var/www/html/uploads/tmp/" + file)

I see that `appveyor.yml` is present, but CI itself is not enabled. Somebody from project owners should register with https://ci.appveyor.com and enable testing.

This is needed to get automatic Windows wheels to install https://github.com/sdg-mit/gitless on this platform.

Here's a sample:
```
                                Second from gmail

2014-10-17 11:28 GMT+03:00 Postmaster <
postmaster@sandboxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx.mailgun.org>:

> First from site
>
```

Quotation ``SPLITTER_PATTERNS`` from ``talon.quotations`` need to be adjusted. In this particular case no new pattern is needed instead one of the following needs to be fixed:

```
re.compile("(\d+/\d+/\d+|\d+\.\d+\.\d+).*@", re.VERBOSE)
```
```
re.compile('\S{3,10}, \d\d? \S{3,10} 20\d\d,? \d\d?:\d\d(:\d\d)?'
               '( \S+){3,6}@\S+:')
```
Consider merging them into one.

My company ran into an issue with some Celery task running in the background that called something that was using Bleach that was throwing an error. Instead of getting a useful traceback, we got the "No handlers could be found for logger "bleach"" exception. 

Our bad, for sure, but it added thousands of lines to our Celery log that we needed to hunt down, and is easily preventable if you simply add a NullHandler to the logging.

See also: http://docs.python-guide.org/en/latest/writing/logging/#logging-in-a-library

issue_title: Problem including BatchNorm after Dense layers
issue_body:
I am trying to include BatchNormalization layers after dense layers, much like the paper DeepSpeech2 implements (next I am going to implement BN on the LSTMs). In summay, I am using the following implementation of batchnorm inside the ```BiRNN``` definition:

```python
    layer_1 = tf.layers.batch_normalization(inputs=layer_1, momentum=FLAGS.batchnorm_momentum, training=training_ph)
```

where ```training_ph``` is a boolean placeholder set to ```True``` during training, and ```False``` otherwise; and ```batchnorm_momentum=0.9```. I've also passing the updateops for session during training (on line https://github.com/mozilla/DeepSpeech/blob/master/DeepSpeech.py#L1637):


```python
update_ops = tf.get_collection(tf.GraphKeys.UPDATE_OPS)
 _, _, current_step, batch_loss, batch_report = session.run([train_op, update_ops ,global_step, loss, report_params], **extra_params)
```

Now to the problem: it seems that it works on training (loss going down), but it seems bugged in validation. As batchnorm is behaving differently for training and validation, the ```training_ph``` appears to be correct, and I suspect that the params of batchnorm (```moving_mean``` and ```moving_variance```) are not being properly updated.

issue_title: Add 'in-testsuite' flag to the bug analysis module
is_safe_url() got an unexpected keyword argument 'host' (Django 2.1)
issue_body: In the [Django deprecation timeline](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/dev/internals/deprecation/#deprecation-removed-in-2-1), the `host` parameter to `is_safe_url` was removed. This project currently passes `host` to determine the `next` url, breaking support for Django 2.1.

issue_body:
When running `nteract foo.ipynb` at the command line, I just get a splash screen and nothing else seems to happen. Running the actual app seems to work fine though.

PS:  This is a fantastic app and I am so happy that someone is working on it. Love the dark theme 👍

#  Description

- It's nearly (see below) impossible to have a notebook working with an haskell kernel

# Reproduce

1. Installation of haskell
2. Installation of ihaskell
3. Try creating an haskell notebook with jupyter: `stack exec jupyter -- notebook` OK.
4. List every kernel installed OK: 
```
> jupyter kernelspec list --json
{
  "kernelspecs": {
    "haskell": {
        [...]
    },
    "python3": {
        [...]
    }
}
```
5. Launch nteract with haskell kernel: `nteract --kernel haskell` KO. Opens with bottom left `Python 3 | idle` and logs `Logging kernel python3`
6. Switch from Python runtime to Haskell KO. Go to Runtime > Select Haskell. Logs `Logging kernel haskell`. Bottom left is showing `Python 3 | not connected`. 
7. Restart Haskell runtime KO. Logs 
```
Kernel restarted
Kernel undefined has been restarted
ERROR: {}
```

Devtools: 
```
app.js:1 Error: launchKernelByNameEpic requires a kernel name
```

Everything looks like haskell kernel is properly detected by nteract (because it's proposed in the runtime selection menu) but I don't know why the kernel name is undefined in this context because it seems it's not the case when listing the kernels with the command line 4 above: `> jupyter kernelspec list --json`

# Why am I feeling confused?

1. Because my notebooks are working perfectly fine with Jupyter. 
2. I have try 2 different computers (OS.X Sierra 10.12.6 each), and try re-installing and ugrading  everything from haskell to python and nteract.
3. Because I have managed - no idea how - to make my notebooks work twice with nteract. I've seen one time the information `haskell | connected`, with all the snippet working fine. One time was: 
    - Not working before lunch
    - Locking computer
    - Working after lunch for a couple of minutes
4. When the haskell kernel was properly connected, closing and re-opening nteract would make my notebooks NOT working again (same error than explained above)

# Disclaimer

I am a Jupyter/Nteract beginner. Maybe I'm doing something wrong. There is not a lot of documentation and information about nteract online that's why it's possible I've missed some information.

Thanks for your help.

Switch to [react-codemirror](https://github.com/JedWatson/react-codemirror). It's more active and both @jdfreder and @Carreau wondered why we weren't on it. Relieve us from the choice I made earlier and switch to the new component.

Below a GIF for a notebook that has (very) long lines, with some indented arguments, etc. When you make the window narrower the formatting of the lines gets worse and worse. Besides arguing over what the correct length of a line is, are there ideas how to make it less horrible? I think my other editors end up scrolling horizontally. The browser based notebook does horizontal scrolling per cell.

![code-line-breaks](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1448859/31043419-6caa35e6-a5bb-11e7-92ce-3a0c287c8762.gif)

Currently `kernelspecs.findAll()` (**Edit:** and `kernelspecs.find()` are) called multiple times throughout the code base. (on startup, on menu init, on kernel launch via spawnteract).

Since it is a promise I think we should only call it once (on startup)  and store the kernel specs in a variable which then can be used throughout the code base.

This should improve performance everywhere kernel specs are used.
Also it would make it possible to very easily add additional kernel specs of bundled kernels in one central place.

E.g. [`ikernel`](https://github.com/rgbkrk/ikernel) could be simply bundled (as a npm dependency) by adding the following spec on startup:

```json
{
  "argv": [
    "path to executable, accessible with `process.execPath`",
    "path to node_modules/ikernel",
    "{connection_file}"
  ],
  "display_name": "ikernel (Node.js)",
  "language": "javascript",
  "env": {
    "ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE": 1
  }
}
```

robot.py:49: in disabled

    self.fake_time.increment_time_by(s)
/home/travis/virtualenv/python3.5.2/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pyfrc/test_support/fake_time.py:215: in increment_time_by
    self._wake_children(time)
/home/travis/virtualenv/python3.5.2/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pyfrc/test_support/fake_time.py:93: in _wake_children
    child_threads = list(self._child_threads.items())
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 
self = <WeakKeyDictionary at 0x7fbe41e7c438>
    def items(self):
        with _IterationGuard(self):
>           for wr, value in self.data.items():

I think the only viable way to prevent the dictionary from changing is by adding a separate lock, calling `weakref.finalize` on any thread we add to the dictionary, and then take that lock during finalization? issue_title: JavaExample: ArrayExample1 fails with "Argument was not an array constant"
issue_body:
I don't know what could cause this, as I've just started looking around at the Java API.
## Reproduction
```shell
git clone https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3
cd z3
python scripts/mk_make.py --java
cd build
make
make examples
LD_LIBRARY_PATH=. java -cp com.microsoft.z3.jar:. JavaExample
````
## Expected
For all the tests to pass and for a normal exit.

## Actual
The program hits an exception when it hits line 389, which is in ArrayExample1.

The last lines of the output:
```
Status: SATISFIABLE
Model = (define-fun x () Int
  7719)
(define-fun MyArray () (Array Int (_ BitVec 32))
  (lambda ((x!1 Int)) #x0000002a))
(define-fun f ((x!0 Int)) Int
  (- 7596))
Z3 Managed Exception: Argument was not an array constant
Stack trace: 
com.microsoft.z3.Z3Exception: Argument was not an array constant
	at com.microsoft.z3.Model.getFuncInterp(Model.java:95)
	at JavaExample.arrayExample1(JavaExample.java:389)
	at JavaExample.main(JavaExample.java:2292)
```

## Version
Z3 [version 4.8.0 - 64 bit]

The OCaml API uses Z3's reference counting by registering a finalizer (which is called upon a major garbage collection of OCaml) with all Z3 native objects:

```ocaml
type z3_native_object = {
  m_ctx : context ;
  mutable m_n_obj : Z3native.ptr ;
  inc_ref : Z3native.z3_context -> Z3native.ptr -> unit;
  dec_ref : Z3native.z3_context -> Z3native.ptr -> unit }
```

Upon freeing a ``z3_native_object``, the finalizer uses ``dec_ref`` to decrement Z3's reference count for the ``m_n_obj``. If it drops to zero, the object is freed by Z3.

Now a pattern in the bindings is as follows (e.g. for simplify):
```ocaml
 let simplify ( x : expr ) ( p : Params.params option ) = match p with
    | None -> expr_of_ptr (Expr.gc x) (Z3native.simplify (gnc x) (gno x))
    | Some pp -> expr_of_ptr (Expr.gc x) (Z3native.simplify_ex (gnc x) (gno x) (z3obj_gno pp))
```
Assume a call to ``simplify`` with some term and no parameters.
When ``expr_of_ptr (Expr.gc x) (Z3native.simplify (gnc x) (gno x))`` gets evaluated,
``gnc x`` and ``gno x`` are evaluated to obtain the native pointers before calling ``Z3native.simplify``.
If the garbage collector performs a major collection before the call to ``Z3native.simplify``, the OCaml value ``x`` might have already become unreachable: This occurs if ``x`` is not used at any later point in the program. For the simplify function, this might actually occur.
In such a garbage collection, value ``x`` is freed and its finalizer decrements Z3's reference counter on its native pointer (i.e. the pointer that has previously been obtained in ``gno x``).
If the reference pointer drops to zero, Z3 frees the term and ``simplify`` is called with an invalid pointer.

If the simplify function (which is only one example - the same behavior occurs in many other functions in the OCaml API) is rewritten to force a garbage collection as follows,

```ocaml
  let simplify ( x : expr ) ( p : Params.params option ) = match p with
    | None ->
      let ctx = Expr.gc x in
      let nc = gnc x in
      let no = gno x in
      Gc.full_major ();
      expr_of_ptr ctx (Z3native.simplify nc no)
    | Some pp -> expr_of_ptr (Expr.gc x) (Z3native.simplify_ex (gnc x) (gno x) (z3obj_gno pp))
```

a segmentation fault can be provoked by a simple test program (if built as native-code executable):
```ocaml
let testfun () =
  let ctx = Z3.mk_context [] in
  let term = Z3.BitVector.mk_add ctx
      (Z3.BitVector.mk_numeral ctx "2" 16)
      (Z3.BitVector.mk_numeral ctx "3" 16)
  in
  let simpler_term = Z3.Expr.simplify term None in
  Printf.printf "The simplified term is %s.\n" (Z3.Expr.to_string simpler_term)

let _ =
  ignore (Z3.Log.open_ "Z3.log");
  Printf.printf "Testing OCaml API...\n";
  testfun ()
```

Note that if the term ``term`` is used after the invocation of ``Z3.Expr.simplify``,
the garbage collector does not free it, and no segfault occurs:

```ocaml
let testfun () =
  let ctx = Z3.mk_context [] in
  let term = Z3.BitVector.mk_add ctx
      (Z3.BitVector.mk_numeral ctx "2" 16)
      (Z3.BitVector.mk_numeral ctx "3" 16)
  in
  let simpler_term = Z3.Expr.simplify term None in
  Printf.printf "The simplified term is %s.\n" (Z3.Expr.to_string simpler_term);
  Printf.printf "The original term was %s.\n" (Z3.Expr.to_string term)

let _ =
  ignore (Z3.Log.open_ "Z3.log");
  Printf.printf "Testing OCaml API...\n";
  testfun ()
```

Am I correctly suspecting a bug in the way, reference counting is implemented in the OCaml API?
The above example uses ``simplify``, but there a plenty of other functions in the API that extract
native pointers from OCaml values such that the latter might become garbage collected, and their native objects being freed before they are used in a Z3 native call.

All of the above has been tested with Z3 at commit e88ac377. The problem might be related to issue #367. A try at fixing the issue is available at https://github.com/martin-neuhaeusser/z3

propagate bit-vector bounds by simplifying implied or contradictory bounds.

issue_title: Refactor tzwin as a tzrange subclass
issue_body:
When working on #249, I realized that all the "time zone" parts of `tzwinbase` are essentially just a different implementation of `tzrange`. I think it's probably a good idea to refactor the library such that `tzrange` is the base class for `tzwin`.

This, along with #253, can be part of a general unification of all the interface of the time zone classes.

```
>>> import datetime
>>> import dateutil.zoneinfo
>>> tz = dateutil.zoneinfo.gettz('Pacific/Apia')

issue_title: Better error message if `abi` is not provided for contract
issue_body:
* Version: 3.x and 4.x

### What was wrong?

In `3.x` if no ABI is provided when instantiating a contract instance you would get an unhelpful error message as seen here: https://github.com/ethereum/web3.py/issues/696

In `4.x` this type of error won't occur, however, the situation can still be approved because the `contract.functions` API simply won't have any accessible functions.

### How can it be fixed?

I don't think we need to fix `3.x` but we *could* provide a simple patch to raise a better error message if `self.abi` is `None`.

In `4.x` I think that we can improve the situation.  Maybe accessing `contract.functions` when `abi is None` should raise an exception that no contract ABI was provided (same for accessing `contract.events`).

issue_title: Specific warning message for ambiguous contract function calls
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

When calling a contract function, if the types of the arguments are ambiguous, so there are more than one matching methods, the error message is confusing: "Function invocation failed due to improper argument encoding".

For example, calling a contract that has both `func1(string)` and `func1(bytes)` with `contract.functions.func1('yo').call()`.

### How can it be fixed?

A separate message for this case, like "multiple functions of the same name match this call". Plus we should give a way to unambiguously call a specific method (maybe by providing the function signature). Something like:

```
func1str = contract.functions['func1(string)']
func1str('yo').call()
```

This is an extension of the work in: #628

issue_title: Deprecate and remove `force_text` and `force_bytes` and dependent functions.
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

The `force_bytes` and `force_text` functions are bad.  They use `latin1` encoding due to the fact that `utf8` cannot encode all of the bytes values between 0-255.  This has caused much confusion given that `utf8` is the *default* encoding that most people expect.

### How can it be fixed?

Fully deprecate the *automatic* coercion of `text -> bytes` and `bytes -> text` including all of the functions which use the derivative functions like `coerce_return_to_*`, and `coerce_args_to_*`.

Introduce new functions `to_text`, `to_bytes` and maybe others, pulled from [Web3.py in `web3.utils.encoding`](https://github.com/ethereum/web3.py/blob/39ef04b30456d37bd5473bd9fecfd9def1c1e6f4/web3/utils/encoding.py#L123-L224).

Any function which previously dependend on the `coerce_args_to_*` should then be migrated to the new call format `fn(primitive=None, hexstr=None, text=None)` format to remove implicit type conversions. 

Any function which depends on `coerce_return_to*` should be converted to do explicit conversions.

Steps to reproduce:

The tests fail with error messages like:
```
FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '…/html5lib-python/html5lib/tests/testdata/encoding/chardet/test_big5.txt'
```

The reason is that `git clone` does not clone/checkout submodules by default. The fix is to run `git submodule update --init`.

When finding `html5lib/tests/testdata` empty, the test harness should print an appropriate warning with instructions.
A lot of PRs fail to report a result back because of Landscape. Let's just replace it with running a fixed version of pylint on Travis/tox.

Both the py2 and new py3 support for the `no_proxy` env var use a simply `endswith` check in `bypass_host`.

There are no standards about the behaviour of no_proxy env var, but `endswith` is probably not what you want here.

If domain starts with a dot (example: .example.com): 
- Use `endswith` to match any subdomain (`foo.example.com` should match)
- Remove the dot and do an exact match (`example.com` should also match)

If domain does not start with a dot (example: example.com): 
- It should be an exact match.

My understanding is that this code will match wrong cases:

```
no_proxy='bar.com'
bypass_host('foobar.com') # TRUE! 
```
Might be a good idea to add a test for it.

See this thread: curl/curl#1208

Even the curl people are struggling with understanding / documenting the correct behaviour. There does not seem to be an authoritative document about it.

issue_title: `expires_in` must be an integer, not a string in IMPLICIT
