issue_title: non standard bps rate support for FreeBSD and others
issue_body:
pyserial inherits the Bxxxx defines and wants a special function for other bps rates.
FreeBSD has no special functions, because the standard functions for Bxxx defines already supports direct bps rates.
The Bxxxx defines are just 1:1 numeric representations.
E.g.:
#define B9600   9600
speed_t is signed 32 bit.
Just setting c_ispeed/c_ospeed to e.g. 250000 will work fine.

issue_title: miniterm.py fails on Windows Subsystem for Linux due to TIOCSTI
issue_body:
On [Windows Subsystem for Linux](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/commandline/wsl/about) the TIOCSTI ioctl isn't supported so Console.cancel fails like:
```
File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/serial/tools/miniterm.py", line 165, in cancel
  fcntl.ioctl(self.fd, termios.TIOCSTI, b'\0')
IOError: [Errno 25] Inappropriate ioctl for device
```
I submitted https://github.com/Microsoft/BashOnWindows/issues/2332. But upon investigation this doesn't seem like a great ioctl to use given security issues with it and a general lack of use (googling it mainly gives articles saying it shouldn't be in linux). So not sure adding it to WSL is the best solution and an alternate solution within pyserial would seem better if possible but not sure how best to do that.
This ioctl was introduced in https://github.com/pyserial/pyserial/commit/cab3dabc81ecbf010d81ff38433db99c1f6d667c to replace the previous select based solution.
Happy to hack at it but pointers would be helpful.

issue_title: Don't use absolute paths in `as_line()` output unless we were given absolute paths.
Finding best matches in a list gives wrong results.
issue_body:
The `process.extract` function doesn't seem to handle capitalised queries well with some scorers:

``` python
>>>fuzzywuzzy.fuzz.partial_ratio('Santa Ana','Santa Ana')
100
>>>fuzzywuzzy.process.extract('Santa Ana', ['Santa Ana', 'Manta'], scorer=fuzzywuzzy.fuzz.partial_ratio)
[('Manta', 80), ('Santa Ana', 77)]
```

This is because [here](https://github.com/seatgeek/fuzzywuzzy/blob/master/fuzzywuzzy/process.py#L84) the choice string is processed but the query string is not:

``` python
>>>fuzzywuzzy.process.extract('santa ana', ['Santa Ana', 'Manta'], scorer=fuzzywuzzy.fuzz.partial_ratio)
[('Santa Ana', 100), ('Manta', 80)]
```

With some other scorers (e.g., WRatio) things work fine because those processes both strings internally anyway.

A workaround I use right now is:

``` python
>>>fuzzywuzzy.process.extract('Santa Ana', ['Santa Ana', 'Manta'], scorer=fuzzywuzzy.fuzz.partial_ratio, processor=lambda x:x)
[('Santa Ana', 100), ('Manta', 80)]
```

We've been using fuzzywuzzy quite successfully for a while but ran into this strange issue:

```
from fuzzywuzzy import fuzz
print round(fuzz.token_set_ratio("Recruiting Partner", "Tooling Planner"))
```

Returns a match  of 61.0. If someone could illuminate us to why this is (we might be missing something!) that'd be great. Starting to peer at the code now

Tried with both 0.3.1 and 0.4

Thanks

issue_title: Logging and swallowing of exceptions causes false-postitive test results
issue_body:
https://github.com/yunojuno/django-side-effects/blob/0c1a01f029a4862babcb0820b02ea3b59a9407fc/side_effects/registry.py#L94  swallows all exceptions and sends them to the `logger` as an `exception()`.

This is arguably OK in production, insomuch as a site with properly configured logging will notice this and inform the maintainers who will fix it ASAP. However, unit tests don't benefit from this, and so will still pass, even though the side effect for a decorated method or function is failing -- denying developers who aren't reading their test run output closely the chance to fix it before it hits production.

As an example, here's some output from a a test which uses a side-effect to send an email, which fails because the template is missing, but which passes in test terms, because the exception is caught. 

```
test_submit_something_to_someone (myproj.tests.apps.myapp.test_models.USFreelancerExternalComplianceStateChangingTests) ... ERROR    Error running side_effect function 'myproj.apps.myapp.side_effects.email.submit_something_to_someone'
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/python_user/.pyenv/versions/3.6.1/lib/python3.6/site-packages/side_effects/registry.py", line 92, in _run_func
    func(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/app/myproj/apps/myapp/side_effects/email.py", line 34, in submit_something_to_someone
    context={}
  File "/app/myproj/apps/messaging/email2.py", line 44, in send_mail
    email_queue.enqueue(_send_mail, template_name, context, **kwargs)
  File "/home/python_user/.pyenv/versions/3.6.1/lib/python3.6/site-packages/rq/queue.py", line 280, in enqueue
    job_id=job_id, at_front=at_front, meta=meta)
  File "/home/python_user/.pyenv/versions/3.6.1/lib/python3.6/site-packages/django_rq/queues.py", line 60, in enqueue_call
    return self.original_enqueue_call(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/home/python_user/.pyenv/versions/3.6.1/lib/python3.6/site-packages/django_rq/queues.py", line 56, in original_enqueue_call
    return super(DjangoRQ, self).enqueue_call(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/home/python_user/.pyenv/versions/3.6.1/lib/python3.6/site-packages/rq/queue.py", line 232, in enqueue_call
    job = self.enqueue_job(job, at_front=at_front)
  File "/home/python_user/.pyenv/versions/3.6.1/lib/python3.6/site-packages/rq/queue.py", line 307, in enqueue_job
    job = self.run_job(job)
  File "/home/python_user/.pyenv/versions/3.6.1/lib/python3.6/site-packages/rq/queue.py", line 237, in run_job
    job.perform()
  File "/home/python_user/.pyenv/versions/3.6.1/lib/python3.6/site-packages/rq/job.py", line 560, in perform
    self._result = self._execute()
  File "/home/python_user/.pyenv/versions/3.6.1/lib/python3.6/site-packages/rq/job.py", line 566, in _execute
    return self.func(*self.args, **self.kwargs)
  File "/app/myproj/apps/messaging/email2.py", line 62, in _send_mail
    email = template.create_message(context, **kwargs)
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'create_message'
ok
```

So, because that AttributeError is swallowed even though it's complaining about something that effectively breaks production, I'd call that a false positive.

Any chance we can have a `settings.SIDE_EFFECTS_RAISE_EXCEPTIONS` config option or similar, so that tests (and production if you want it) can be fail hard?

Thanks.

We renamed some folders and the folders are having to do a full upload with the new names. I was under the impression before signing up that renaming folders was fine as a fingerprint at both ends was used to know its the same file, and just update the server.

According to the following page, if a file is renamed or moved, it should have a matching fingerprint and just be updated on the server rather than being uploaded again.

Could it be that our sync script is not using the right parameters on the CLI tool or is there a bug in the CLI?

Our content team need to rename a lot of folders, in total these folders contain about 8tb of data, which will take a good 1.5-2 weeks non stop to upload again when renamed, plus the manual job of deleting the old folders from the backblaze b2 server. 

Thank you.

Clint.

https://help.backblaze.com/hc/en-us/articles/217666638-What-Happens-If-I-Move-or-Rename-a-File-

"Our software contains a process called "deduplication" where files are digitally fingerprinted (checksummed) before they are sent to the server. When a file's fingerprint (SHA-1 checksum) matches an already backed up file, but it's renamed or moved (including drive to drive), it's simply updated at the servers, rather than re-transmitted.

You may see these files queued for back up, but once any given file is at the front of the queue, it will be checksummed, compared against the existing backup, and "deduplicate" resulting in it being updated on the server rather than re-uploading"

from raven import Client

- [] The manuscript file should be formatted as double-spaced, single-column text without justification
- [] Acknowledgements should be brief, and should not include thanks to anonymous referees and editors, or effusive comments. Grant or contribution numbers may be acknowledged.
- [] References are numbered sequentially as they appear in the text, tables, figure legends and online Methods. Only one publication is given for each number. Only papers that have been published or accepted by a named publication or recognized preprint server should be in the numbered list.
- [] Published conference abstracts, numbered patents and research datasets that have been assigned a digital object identifier may be included in the reference list. URLs for web sites should be cited parenthetically in the text, not in the reference list; articles in formal, peer-reviewed online journals should be included in the reference list.
- [] Nature Biotechnology uses standard Nature referencing style. All authors should be included in reference lists unless there are more than five, in which case only the first author should be given, followed by 'et al.'. Authors should be listed last name first, followed by a comma and initials (followed by full stops) of given names. Article titles should be in Roman text, the first word of the title should be capitalized and the title written exactly as it appears in the work cited, ending with a full stop.
- [] Figure legends for Articles or Letters begin with a brief title for the whole figure and continue with a short description of each panel and the symbols used, focusing on describing what is shown in the figure and de-emphasizing methodological details. The meaning of all error bars and how they were calculated should be described. Each legend should total no more than 250 words.
- [] Please submit tables at the end of your text document (in Word or TeX/LaTeX, as appropriate). Tables that include statistical analysis of data should describe their standards of error analysis and ranges in a table legend.
- [] Figure lettering should be in a clear, sans-serif typeface (for example, Helvetica); if possible, the same typeface in approximately the same font size should be used for all figures in a paper.
- [] Figures divided into parts should be labelled with a lower-case bold a, b, and so on, in the same type size as used elsewhere in the figure.
- [] In legends, please use visual cues rather than verbal explanations, such as "open red triangles".
- [] Authors are encouraged to consider the needs of colorblind readers (a substantial minority of the male population) when choosing colors for figures. 
- [] Supplementary information is not copy-edited by Nature Biotechnology, so authors should ensure that it is clearly and succinctly presented, and that the style and terminology conform with the rest of the paper. Please note that modification of supplementary information after the paper is published requires a formal correction, so authors are encouraged to check their supplementary information carefully before submitting the final version.
- [] The number of Supplementary Figures should not exceed the number of main figures in the article, and each Supplementary Figure should fit one printable 8.5 x 11 inch page. While we would encourage authors to be selective in including as other types of supplemental data, such as Supplementary Tables and Videos, no restrictions will currently apply to these data types.
- [] Designate each item as Supplementary Table, Figure, Video, Audio, Notes, Data, Discussion or Equations. Number Supplementary Tables and Figures as, for example, "Supplementary Table 1". This numbering should be separate from that used in tables and figures appearing in the main printed article. Supplementary Notes should not be numbered and can have an optional title. Please provide a title for Supplementary Tables and a title and a caption for Supplementary Figures, Supplementary Video and Supplementary Notes.
- [] Refer to each piece of supplementary information at least once within the text of the main article, at the appropriate point(s). Be sure to include the word "Supplementary" each time one is mentioned. Please do not refer to individual panels of supplementary figures.

- [] Nature Biotechnology uses American English spelling

Any changes to the author list after submission, such as a change in the order of the authors, or the deletion or addition of authors, needs to be approved by every author.
- [] The Nature Research journals do not prescribe the kinds of contributions that warrant authorship but encourage transparency by publishing author contributions statements.
- [] Before submission, the corresponding author ensures that all authors are included in the author list, its order has been agreed by all authors, and that all authors are aware that the paper was submitted.

issue_title: Use entities data to generate test case parsers dynamically
issue_body:
The [`entities.py`](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/blob/master/openfisca_france/entities.py) module contains data about entities and roles of persons. It should be the single source of truth for these data.

But at some other places roles are hard-coded like in the [test case validator](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/blob/master/openfisca_france/scenarios.py#L127) where we see `foyers_fiscaux = ` or `declarants =`, etc..

I think they could be generated dynamically, based on the `entities.py` data, then moved to the Core as they will become generic.

This could be useful for Tunisia and Senegal and reduce the boilerplate when initiating a new country.

I don't intend to start doing it for the moment, this issue is for the record.

issue_title: Create collection and resource under virtual to allow access to policies
issue_body:
The virtual resource currently does not have SDK access to its policies. Therefore we cannot perform CRUD operations on these policies. We need to add the collection for Policies_s and the resource Policies. The collection is shown below:

{
  "kind": "tm:ltm:virtual:policies:policiescollectionstate",
  "selfLink": "https://localhost/mgmt/tm/ltm/virtual/~Common~test/policies?ver=12.1.0",
  "items": []
}

This will require some refactor work in the Policy module, mostly surrounding publish and legacy keywords. We will strive to preserve the old behavior and we will attempt to do the right thing when these types of arguments are given for bigip versions that are less than 12.1.0.

I modified your "hello-world" example to work with gevent.  In case you need a gevent example to include with the package, the following worked for me:

import gevent
from haigha.connection import Connection
from haigha.message import Message
 
connection = Connection(
  user='guest', password='guest',
  vhost='/', host='localhost',
 heartbeat=None, debug=True, transport="gevent")
 
def consumer(msg):
  print "CONSUMER: MESSAGE RECEIVED: ", msg 
  gevent.kill(g)
  #NOTE: haigha's hello-world example had connection.close()
  # here (instead of our geven.kill(g) statement).  The close()
  # resulted in deadlock when running with gevent as we're here.
  # Even Ctrl-c wouldn't budge this deadlock! 
 
ch = connection.channel()
 
ch.exchange.declare('test_exchange', 'direct', auto_delete=True)
 
ch.queue.declare('test_queue', auto_delete=True)
 
ch.queue.bind('test_queue', 'test_exchange', 'test_key')
 
ch.basic.consume('test_queue', consumer)
 
ch.basic.publish( Message('body', application_headers={'hello':'world'}),
   'test_exchange', 'test_key' )
 
 
def readFrames(conn):
  while True:
    conn.read_frames()
 
 
g = gevent.spawn(readFrames, conn=connection)
 
result = g.get()
print "readFrames result: ", repr(result)
 
connection.close()

* Supporting wallet entirely on portal *

To support using portal's wallet for both identity and coin storage, core needs to support an API endpoint for generating transaction templates that can be signed and sent by portal.
See issue #363 

* Supporting core wallet via the API *

If we move forward with using the core wallet instead of the portal wallet, we will need an API that lets users work with wallets. They need to be able to create, delete, backup, and restore wallets.

Importantly, these methods should require the wallet password as a parameter _not_ the API key.

Currently, the API server requires the wallet password on startup. The API server should be able to start without yet knowing the wallet password, and fail on endpoints that require the password if it isn't entered yet.

Robert

issue_title: Implementar función para remover un dataset de un CKAN / Andino vía API
issue_body:
**Contexto**

La administración de la federación crónica de datasets de nodos originales hacia un nodo integrador, así como la propia administración de ese nodo integrador, requiere la habilidad programática de borrar datasets de nodos CKAN.

**Implementar**

En el módulo nuevo creado al implementar #89 se escribieron funciones para manejar metadatos de un CKAN. Una de las cuales (`push_dataset_to_ckan`) fue _bindeada_ al objeto `DataJson` del `core.py` porque está pensada desde la perspectiva del catálogo original (cómo empujo este dataset de un catálogo original a un catálogo integrador).

Esta función `remove_dataset_from_ckan` se escribiría en el nuevo módulo, pero sin _bindearla_ al objeto (ya que se usa en forma independiente de la existencia de cualquier catálogo original: sólo busca borrar un dataset de un Portal CKAN a través del uso de distintos parámetros opcionales.

+ `identifier`: se borra del CKAN aquel dataset que se encuentre y tenga ese identificador.

+ `organization`: se borran del CKAN todos los datasets que se encuentren y pertenezcan a esa organización.

+ `publisher_name`: se borran del CKAN todos los datasets que se encuentren y tengan ese responsable de publicación en sus metadatos de `publisher_name`.

issue_title: Question about Joiners management in Commissioner
issue_body:
I have a question regarding Commissioner and Joiners management. Current implementation seems to only support manual Joiner removal. It seems quite inconvinient to remove it manually anytime Joiner attaches, otherwise the buffer for Joiners gets filled up easily.

I would like to introduce some automation to the Joiner removal process, and I see two options to implement it:
1. One option is to implement it directly in Commissioner class and remove Joiner after succesfully sending Joiner Finalize Response. As I checked the code, it shall not impact the possible retransmissions of this message. Additionally, a timer could be added to remove Joiners after some time, in case commissioning failed / did not happen at all.
2. Second option is to provide some callback funtion to the application, and call it whenever Commissioning process ends. The application would be responsible for removing Joiners and possible timeout handling.

Personally I would opt for option 1, as it seems neat and would not require to modify for example a NCP code, but I would like to see your opinion.

issue_title: importing pex.variables will may look at disk and update state
issue_body:
https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/blob/master/pex/variables.py#L36

When a module imports variables it creates an ENV object which checks the env variables and stores them.  It also may read files from disk.  In most cases this is probably an unexpected side effect.  Since many modules inside pex import variables the user is often more removed from this effect since they may not be directly importing variables.

Proposed solution is to change Variables to load values lazily on first access.  This will be a change in behavior and may impact users who implicitly rely on this behavior when using pex as a library.

    $ pex -v -r requirements/dev.txt -c ipython -o dist/dev.pex
    $ PYTHONPATH=~/code/project ~/code/project/dist/dev.pex    # absolute path so this works regardless of current directory

It would be great to see a docs section about development. Is this the Right Way (tm) to use pex in a development workflow? If so, call it out explicitly. If instead there are caveats, or if only the deployment use case is supported / recommended, this should also be explicit.

issue_title: investigate lmdb dep issue potentially related to CI hangs.
issue_body:
Ity saw an issue on her laptop recently where `pants` was segfaulting when spinning up the daemon, which wound up related to a bad `lmdb` bdist in her pants venv.

It seems like this situation is now potentially presenting itself in CI occasionally - with the symptom being hangs on both the multiprocessing engine test (and since reenabled yesterday, the pantsd integration test).

clearing the travis cache and restarting the problem shards resolves the issue - which leads me to believe this is related to the lmdb dep problem. we should take a look at this and attempt to understand how it's happening and how to resolve.

issue_title: Pants context scoped options that have a prefix matching a scope are always assigned to the prefix
issue_body:
That plus lazy parsing means that arbitrary flags that match any scope will be allowed and ignored. This becomes a particularly annoying issue if a task registers an option that has a prefix that matches a scope that isn't executed.
eg --jvm-options will be silently ignored in a bunch of places if it's referenced like `./pants resolve.ivy --jvm-options='1 2 3' my-target` and not like `./pants resolve.ivy --resolve-ivy-jvm-options='1 2 3' my-target`.

It also means that pants will accept and ignore any flag as long as the scope in question is never evaluated. For example:

`./pants test --targets-whatever=1000 my-test-target`

I think we should either make it so that the context scope's flags have precedence over global flags, or enforce on registration that there are no overlapping scopes. Enforcing would be a bit more difficult.

issue_title: Python Thrift caching issue with included Thrift files
issue_body:
Test case available on the `thrift-include` branch of https://github.com/wyattanderson/pantstest/tree/thrift-include

### Problem
Under-invalidation when modifying transitive Thrift dependencies of a Thrift target. We have a Thrift API in `A.thrift` that imports `B.thrift` and `C.thrift`. `B` and `C` are consumable via separate targets as well. `A` depends on `B` and `C` in the BUILD file in addition to importing them:

```
python_thrift_library(
    name='A',
    sources=[
        'A.thrift',
    ],
    dependencies=[
        ':B',
        ':C',
    ]
)

python_thrift_library(
    name='B',
    sources=[
        'B.thrift',
    ]
)

python_thrift_library(
    name='C',
    sources=[
        'C.thrift',
    ]
)
```

The most reliable reproducer I can find is **in a project with artifact caching enabled, make a change to `C.thrift` and run/build a Python target that depends on `A.thrift`. The changes won't be picked up unless you `clean-all`**.

### Setup
1. Clone the repository
1. `./pants binary src/python:test && dist/test.pex`
1. Expected output: 

        ['C', 'read', 'thrift_spec', 'validate', 'write']
1. Uncomment lines 5-7 in `src/thrift/C.thrift`
1. `./pants binary src/python:test && dist/test.pex`

### Expected output 
    ['C', 'Cba', 'Cfoo', 'Cfoobar', 'read', 'thrift_spec', 'validate', 'write']

### Actual output
    ['C', 'read', 'thrift_spec', 'validate', 'write']

If you run `./pants clean-all`, you get the expected output, which makes me think this is an artifact caching issue. The actual problem we're seeing in production is even stranger: the addition of a new type to the equivalent of the `C.thrift` file doesn't get included in the built PEX.

issue_title: Make sure faulthandler displays crashes even after pytest is shutting down
issue_body: Crashes might happen during garbage collection of objects, well past the point `pytest_unconfigure` was called and pytest has even returned control to `__main__`.

### Environment

* Operating System: RHEL
* Python Version: `$ python --version` 3.6
* How did you install Qgrid: (`pip`, `conda`, or `other (please explain)`) pip install
* Python packages: `$ pip freeze` or `$ conda list` (please include qgrid, notebook, and jupyterlab versions)
[LibraryList.txt](https://github.com/quantopian/qgrid/files/1918787/LibraryList.txt)

* Jupyter lab packages (if applicable): `$ jupyter labextension list`

### Description of Issue
Hi,
I`m loading a dataframe into qgrid:

`qgrid_widget = qgrid.QgridWidget(df=dfCRD)`
`qgrid_widget`

dfCRD has 24 columns which are stacked and unreadable.

* What did you expect to happen?

Wanted columns to have  widths allowing to see the names. Thus qgrid should be scrollable horizonally. 

* What happened instead?
dfCRD has 24 columns which are stacked and unreadable.
### Reproduction Steps

1.
Clean Jupytelab notebook

2.
Loading dfCRD dataframe with cx_oracle
3.
`qgrid_widget = qgrid.QgridWidget(df=dfCRD)`
`qgrid_widget`

### What steps have you taken to resolve this already?

`qgrid_widget = qgrid.QgridWidget(df=dfCRD, show_toolbar=True, options={'forceFitColumns': False, 'defaultColumnWidth': 200
})`
`qgrid_widget`

issue_title: Automating TSV file generation of labels for  HiddenLayer0 of DNN Classifiers
issue_body: Hi Firstly congratulations on Tensorboard, it is fantastic tool (especially when demo-ing to executives). We have been developing AI solutions for a four months now and have built two commercial DNN Classifiers (for stockbroking clients) **Tensorboard Projector and the DNN Classifier** One of the wow moments we get from clients is when we show the learnt embeddings for hiddenlayer_0 in tensorboard with labels. We will perform a T-SNE in front of them and the clients can see the clusters of data that the classifier is using to make it’s decisions. In fact, the client will have a-ha moments when the see the inputs that are clustered together. I am not sure it is obvious to many people how powerful visualising the embeddings are even in a simple 2-3 hidden layer classifier. **Feature Request** Our feature request is to automatically generate the tsv file for hiddenlayer0 for this class of classifer. **Automating TSV file generation for HiddenLayer0** Given most classifiers feature columns are one-hot category vectors, buckets and numeric columns, we have built an automatic tsv generator that 1. Hooks into DNNClassifer using SessionRunHook 2. Grabs the dnn/input_from_feature_columns/input_layer/concat:0 tensor 3. For each column type e.g. categorical, bucket, numeric, embedding a. Creates a label based on categorical_colunn definition I have put the code we use on github (refer to https://github.com/amarjeetsihota/tensorboard.classifier.auto_tsv_file_maker/blob/master/README.md) for your reference,. **Demo** For demo purposes, I have built a simple classifier using the @“Mental Health in Tech Survey” dataset in Kaggle, and saved the created a standalone tensorboard projector github ( click here and choose the “Mental Health Classifer” from the tensor list **Only a suggestion** Feel free to delete this request if it is not in the general direction of the product, as this is for your interest and I don't want to clog up the to-do list unnecessarily. **DevSummit** I attending the summit next week so if you are interested in what we are doing please feel free to get in contact and I will see you there! email amarjeet@agiledevsolutions.com.au (Lead AI consultant and owner)

issue_title: On Windows 10, after starting TensorBoard, pressing Ctrl+C to quit as shown on the screen does not work
issue_body:
I posted this on Stack Overflow a few weeks ago, and did not get any responses:

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/47856093/tensorflow-how-to-stop-tensorboard-from-command-line-in-windows

but the more I think it through, this seems to be a bug.  I'll replicate the desctiption here in case the above link ever breaks, and for convenience:

If I put the following at the end of a TensorFlow Python program:

```
# show the result in TensorBoard
tfFileWriter = tf.summary.FileWriter(os.getcwd())
tfFileWriter.add_graph(sess.graph)
tfFileWriter.close()
```

I get a file in the same directory that is titled something like:

`events.out.tfevents.1513529946.DESKTOP-KR0HCRR`

Then I open a command prompt and enter:

`tensorboard --logdir C:\Users\cdahms\Documents\TensorFlowExample9`

I get the output from the command line:

`TensorBoard 0.1.8 at http://DESKTOP-KR0HCRR:6006 (Press <kbd>CTRL</kbd>+<kbd>C</kbd> to quit)`

If I open a broswer and go to http://localhost:6006 I get the expected TensorBoard graph.

Problem is, if I press CTRL+C the process from the command line does not quit as the TensorBoard output indicates. Here is a screenshot if that would be helpful:

![untitled](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5672876/34643092-2603a04a-f2d3-11e7-8871-ccd462b7607b.png)

I gather the CTRL+C must be for Linux and/or Mac but does not work in Windows. I've also tried X, Ctrl+X, Ctrl+Z, Esc, Crtl+Esc, Ctrl+D, and pretty much every other combination of keystroke to stop a process that I've ever known of.

To clarify I'm using Windows 10 and PyCharm as an editor (I'm not using a Jupyter Notebook).  I'm using Python 3.6.x and Chrome as my browser if that matters.

Also, before somebody responds "just close that command line window and that will end TensorBoard, then open another command line window and enter the command again" yes, of course I understand I can do that, however it would be preferable to be able to stop TensorBoard from the command line and then re-start it after changing a script without having to close/re-open a new command line window.

If anybody could address this concern us Windows users would appreciate it, thanks.

issue_title: Erase spinner used in zhmc CLI once the command is complete
issue_labels: [{'id': 429586455, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/area:%20devenv', 'name': 'area: devenv', 'color': '207de5', 'default': False}, {'id': 429588767, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/resolution:%20fixed', 'name': 'resolution: fixed', 'color': 'cccccc', 'default': False}, {'id': 429588947, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/resolution:%20pursued%20elsewhere', 'name': 'resolution: pursued elsewhere', 'color': 'cccccc', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
### Actual behavior

When using the zhmc CLI in a tty session, a character-based spinner is turning in order to indicate progress. When the command is complete, the last character shown is visible in the output.

When redirecting the output, the spinner is not turning, so that is not a problem for redirection cases.

### Expected behavior

Have the spinner characters erased once the command is complete. This can be achieved in the same way the `click-spinner` package places the next spinner character over the previous one: by printing the backspace character `\b`.

### Execution environment

* zhmcclient version: 0.5.1.dev37
* Operating system (type+version): RHEL 7.2 (issue applies to any)

Hey,

We noticed recently that all of our scripts can't exit. I tracked this down to libhoney.

If you do this in a fresh virtualenv:
```
pip install libhoney==1.0.11 && echo "import libhoney; libhoney.init(); print('hi')" > test.py && python test.py
```
The script will hang. It won't respond to ctrl+c. From `htop` I discovered that `SIGINT` does nothing; however, `SIGKILL`, `SIGQUIT`, and `SIGTERM` will kill the script.

I diagnosed with [hanging_threads](https://gist.github.com/niccokunzmann/6038331) and got this stack trace as the blocking culprit:

```

During the closed beta, I created a certificate for use by a non-webserver using letsencrypt-auto; the `--cert-path` option was honoured.  When renewing (during open beta), the `--cert-path` was ignored.

Is this semantic confusion over the interaction between `certonly` and `--cert-path` during renewal?  I expected that locations where artifacts were placed during install would also be touched during update.  Anything else I should be doing?

```
./letsencrypt-auto certonly \
    --server $closed_beta_url --agree-dev-preview \
    -a standalone -d demo.nats.io --cert-path /etc/nats/tls
```

Renewal was with the same command but with `--server` and `--agree-dev-previews` options dropped.

Notably, the original `--cert-path` has generation numbers in the filenames for the placed certs, the same generation numbers which were updated by this renewal.

I would naively expect the contents of `--cert-path` to look very like the contents of `/etc/letsencrypt/live/demo.nats.io` except with files instead of symlinks.

issue_title: Apache and Nginx plugins should use reload rather than restart
issue_body:
letsencrypt-apache/letsencrypt_apache/configurator.py
>     .. todo:: Try to use reload instead. (This caused timing problems before)                                                                                                    

Our challenge methods are designed to be used without taking down a web server, so we should make sure we actually don't take down the web server to restart.

Note that Nginx has this issue too, noted in https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/pull/950 and worked around.

We need a generic wait_for_challenge method that can be used for both plugins, which will repeatedly attempt to connect to the appropriate port on localhost and ask for the appropriate servername. If the loop times out after, say, five seconds, it should error out and say the configuration to add the challenge failed.

We need a test farm test that creates a release, creating sdists and wheels, and installs these packages on the test farm instances. There were a lot of problems with the `0.2.0` release stemming from:

* Changes to `setup.py` files not being tested in wheels
* Differences in results of `nosetests` when running against real packages.

issue_title: Missing Docs for cert.pem, chain.pem, fullchain.pem, etc
issue_body:
When this goes live, **where will the intermediate certificates be located**?

Searching the docs for `chain.pem` yields [no results](https://letsencrypt.readthedocs.org/en/latest/search.html?q=chain.pem&check_keywords=yes&area=default).

I just [asked on the mailing list](See https://groups.google.com/a/letsencrypt.org/forum/#!topic/client-dev/jE5uK4lPx5g), but it seems it should be an issue on here as well.

Currently we have these files:

* privkey.pem
* cert.pem
* chain.pem
* fullchain.pem

But we don't know what goes in them. With the current dummy certificate it puts the **Root CA** in the `chain.pem` (which seems wrong) and `fullchain.pem` and that the **Server Certificate** goes into `cert.pem` and `fullchain.pem`, but we don't know where the **Intermediate Certificates** go.

This is extremely important because some webservers are lax, others are strict, others barf if you give too much info, others silently fail if you give to little.

I've already written some documentation and demo code based on assumption, but I already know that that's going to blow up in my face if I don't get the details correct.

In order for greatest compatibility with various servers it would seem that we need a file that bundles the server + intermediate certificates, and *excludes* the root certificate. I'm hoping that's what `cert.pem` is intended to be.

Once I know the technical details as to what these files are supposed to represent, I may open another issue to discuss the need for additional files.

issue_title: Convenient way found for letsencrypt with reverse proxy (apache, alias, mod_proxy)
issue_body:
I don't know what the latest standpoint is regarding ports for standalone, because there are so many issues googable regarding reverse proxies, I have absolutely no overview.

The idea was using the apache's Alias directive for e standardized challenge process for all variants of virtualhost configurations. With small tweaks it's also working with proxies.

```
root@server2:~# cat /etc/apache2/conf-enabled/letsencrypt.conf
<IfModule mod_proxy.c>
        ProxyPass /.well-known/acme-challenge !
</IfModule>
Alias /.well-known/acme-challenge /var/www/html/.well-known/acme-challenge

<Directory "/var/www/html/.well-known/acme-challenge">
    Options None
    AllowOverride None
    Require all granted
    AddDefaultCharset off
</Directory>
```

Alias allows us to use this path globally over all virtualhosts.
ProxyPass /.well-known/acme-challenge ! will negate any proxy handling on this path
Directory directive is required with minimal permissions to access this location

```
root@server2:~# cat /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/gitlab.denis.mylastname.me.conf
<VirtualHost *:80>
        ServerName gitlab.denis.mylastname.me
        ErrorLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/error.log
        CustomLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/access.log combined

        AllowEncodedSlashes NoDecode
        #RequestHeader set X-Forwarded-Proto "https"
        #RequestHeader set X-Forwarded-Ssl "on"
        ProxyPreserveHost On

        ProxyPass / http://localhost:8079/
        ProxyPassReverse / http://localhost:8079/
</VirtualHost>
```

As you can see, you can also choose another folder instead of /var/www/html - just use one you don't use anywhere else and adapt accordingly..

```
Requesting root privileges to run with virtualenv: /root/.local/share/letsencrypt/bin/letsencrypt certonly --webroot --renew-by-default --email info@mylastname.me --agree-tos --domains=gitlab.denis.mylastname.me -w /var/www/html --text

IMPORTANT NOTES:
 - Congratulations! Your certificate and chain have been saved at
   /etc/letsencrypt/live/gitlab.denis.mylastname.me/fullchain.pem. Your
   cert will expire on 2016-04-12. To obtain a new version of the
   certificate in the future, simply run Let's Encrypt again.
[...]
```

Here are my thoughts on the near future of PIMS development.

Before tagging v0.3:
- Add a few more readers with associated optional dependencies.
- Remove the janky `plugin` argument from `ImageSequence`, and instead create separate classes (`ImageSequence_skimage`, `ImageSequence_mpl`, `ImageSequence_scipy`) the way we do for the variants of `TiffStack`. As with `TiffStack`, there will be a convenient `ImageSequence` alias pointing at the most reliable variant available.
- Merge #147 with recursive slicing and the new "pipeline" idea.

Then, once v0.3 is tagged, I'd like to make a more serious break change. If the pipelines are working well, I'd like to simplify all the built-in readers by removing `process_func`, `as_grey`, `dtype` as standard arguments. Any of those tasks -- and more -- can be accomplished more transparently and more generally by "pipeline" functions. I'd prefer the reader classes to be as lightweight and simple as possible. (Note that users could still implement their own readers that have a `process_func` arg or whatever they want; I'm just talking about the built-in readers here.)

With that major change in place, then I think it's time to tag 1.0.

▶ Local vars

/home/docker/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/oscar/core/loading.py in _import_module
        return __import__(module_label, fromlist=classnames) ...
▼ Local vars
Variable	Value
classnames	['get_product_search_handler_class']
module_label	'dpm.catalogue.search_handlers'

./dpm/catalogue/search_handlers.py in <module>
    from oscar.apps.catalogue.search_handlers import ProductSearchHandler as OscarProductSearchHandler

    class ProductSearchHandler(OscarProductSearchHandler): ...
        def get_search_queryset(self):
            sqs = super(ProductSearchHandler, self).get_search_queryset()
            return sqs.exclude(num_in_stock=0)
▼ Local vars
Variable	Value
OscarProductSearchHandler	 None
```

It seemed like a circular import or something.  I just now worked out what is wrong ...

In 1.1.1 catalogue/search_handlers.py there is this:

```python
# Deprecated name. TODO: Remove in Oscar 1.2
ProductSearchHandler = deprecated(SolrProductSearchHandler)
```

This is fine if you try and instantiate (i.e. `__call__`) the class. But apparently it breaks any code that tries to inherit from that class.

Once I worked this out I had an easy fix for my app, just import `SolrProductSearchHandler` and inherit from that instead.

...but what happened is not 'deprecation', it was a breaking change - I guess unintentional?

It seems a different decorator is required if 'painless' deprecation of possibly-overridden classes is intended, eg:

```python
def deprecated_cls(cls):
    class NewClass(cls):
        def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
            message = "Class '%s' is deprecated and will be " \
                "removed in the next major version of django-oscar" \
                % cls.__name__
            warnings.warn(message, DeprecationWarning, stacklevel=2)
            super(NewClass, self).__init__(*args, **kwargs)
    return NewClass
```

Steps to reproduce:
1. Build the sandbox as described here: http://django-oscar.readthedocs.org/en/latest/internals/sandbox.html#run-the-sandbox-locally
2. Run `sites/sandbox/manage.py squashmigrations basket 0005`
3. Try to start the server back up `sites/sandbox/manage.py runserver`

As far as I can tell, it's because `basket.Basket` has a `ManyToManyField` to `voucher.Voucher`; `voucher.VoucherApplication` has a `ForeignKey` to `voucher.Voucher` and `order.Order`; and `order.Order` has a `ForeignKey` to `basket.Basket`.

I'm not sure how big of an issue this is, but it seems like there is a lot of complicated interdependence between those 3 apps at the model level.

I'll submit a PR with a proposed fix.

``` python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "sites/sandbox/manage.py", line 10, in <module>
    execute_from_command_line(sys.argv)
...
django.db.migrations.graph.CircularDependencyError: order.0001_initial, basket.0001_squashed_0005_auto_20150604_1450, voucher.0001_initial
```

issue_title: Pass basket line to Range method contains_product
issue_body:
Which reason for passing to Range method `contains_product` only product instance, but not entire basket line, in `can_apply_condition` method of Condition class [here](https://github.com/tangentlabs/django-oscar/blob/master/oscar/apps/offer/models.py#L505)?

``` python
...
product = line.product
return self.range.contains_product(product) and product.is_discountable
...
```

I understand what I can override any method in nested classes of Condition etc. But why it is by default? If reason is Range instance as object of real world is entity what operate not basket lines but products, then I have one example where having of basket line instance in `contains_product` is needed.
1. One table with quantity -> price values and FK to Product. For bulk discount with price by quantity of product rules.
2. On each request basket middleware fetch related objects of basket line.
3. Good place to fetch information about matched rule of price and quantity for current product in basket line is Basket's method `all_lines`.
4. Make own Basket and BasketLine class. Add field `price_by_quantity_rule` to BasketLine which is FK to table from 1 above. Override in Basket following methods – `all_lines` (fetch additional related fields), `add_product` (add related rule in `get_or_create` for new basket line to our table).
5. It's ready for calculate price in my custom Condition and Benefit, all needed information already in basket line without additional queries. But when we calculate range for these discount we need some cycling queries for each product in basket, just because of absent information about basket line.

I am sure it is just one of many examples where this obvious restriction is not good.

issue_title: Wavelet denoising fails on odd-sized dimension images
issue_body:
From @grlee77 https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/pull/2461#issuecomment-273717628

If you truncate the odd-sized dimension from 451 to 450 this issue will not occur.  This is a known issue with odd-sized inputs with `dwtn/idwtn` (https://github.com/PyWavelets/pywt/issues/80)

The proper fix is probably for to have `denoise_wavelet` itself either:
- [ ] raise an error if any non-channels dimension of the input has odd size


@stsievert Would you like to fit that in a new PR?

issue_title: blob_log, blob_dog and blob_doh error when not detecting peaks
issue_body:
## Description

Blob detection with any of the skimage.feature.blob_log, blob_dog or blob_doh fails when no blobs are detected.

```
IndexError                                Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-15-0eabae5c0e37> in <module>()
----> 1 blob_log(a)

/Users/jj/anaconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/skimage/feature/blob.py in blob_log(image, min_sigma, max_sigma, num_sigma, threshold, overlap, log_scale)
    306     lm = local_maxima.astype(np.float64)
    307     # Convert the last index to its corresponding scale value
--> 308     lm[:, 2] = sigma_list[local_maxima[:, 2]]
    309     local_maxima = lm
    310     return _prune_blobs(local_maxima, overlap)

IndexError: index 2 is out of bounds for axis 1 with size 2
```
## Way to reproduce

``` python
from skimage.feature import blob_log
a=np.zeros((100,100))
blob_log(a)
```

I'm on OSX 10.11.6, Python 3.5, scikit-image 0.12.3

## Description
There is some question about the definition h-maxima / h-minima. I found several articles defining them differently. In the original work by Grimaud and Vachier, h-maxima were defined as the regional maxima of the image R_f(f-h) (reconstruction by dilation with mask f and marker f-h). In Soille, h-maxima is defined simply as R_f(f-h). Importantly, the output is thus a filtered version of the image and not a binary image representing the regional maxima. These maxima are then called "extended maxima" in the Soille-version. This nomenclature - albeit not the original one - has been used in MATLAB. 

The question is: should we rather use the MATLAB nomenclature? My feeling is that this would be better, as more people are used to this (both because of being used in MATLAB and in the popular book by Pierre Soille). 

If you have an opinion on this, please share it with me, I will do the necessary modifications.

issue_title: which_python should leverage sys.executable when appropriate
issue_body: `which_python` replicates the behavior of unix `which python` for it's searches… which can be wrong for user environments with complex `$PATH` manipulations.  When "find the path to this python" is desired, `sys.executable` is more reliable, and it should be used for that particular use case.

shed_upload silent if missing .shed.yml include files
issue_body:
My reading of the code suggests that the default ``include`` value is ``**`` (all files next to the ``.shed.yml`` file). I have tried adding an explicit listing of files, e.g.

```
$ more ~/repositories/pico_galaxy/tools/venn_list/.shed.yml
name: venn_list
owner: peterjc
homepage_url: https://github.com/peterjc/pico_galaxy/tools/venn_list
remote_repository_url: https://github.com/peterjc/pico_galaxy/tools/venn_list
description: Draw Venn Diagram (PDF) from lists, FASTA files, etc
long_description: |
  Draw Venn Diagrams for 1, 2 or 3 sets of identifiers as a PDF file.

  Can parse FASTA, FASTQ, SFF or tabular files (taking column one) for identifiers.
  This can be combined with the extensive tabular file filtering and manipulation
  tools within Galaxy to prepare the input data.

  Uses the R/Bioconductor package limma to draw the PDF file, called from Python with
  rpy.

  Uses Biopython to parse SFF files.
categories:
- Graphics
- Sequence Analysis
- Visualization
type: unrestricted
include:
- README.rst
- venn_list_with_typo.py
- venn_list.xml
- tool_dependencies.xml
```

This fails to complain about missing ``venn_list_with_typo.py``:

```
$ planemo shed_upload --tar_only  ~/repositories/pico_galaxy/tools/venn_list 
cp /tmp/tmpx0zfjB shed_upload.tar.gz
$ tar -tzf /tmp/tmpx0zfjB
tool_dependencies.xml
README.rst
venn_list.xml
```

On the positive side, it did not include ``venn_list.py`` because it wasn't asked to (deliberate mismatch with ``venn_list_with_typo.py``).

I want this example to fail, complaining that ``venn_list_with_typo.py`` was listed in the includes, but not found. Ideally the error would list all named but missing files, but just the first missing file would be acceptable.

As an aside, would checking the include files exist also fall under ``planemo lint`` functionality for validating the ``.shed.yml``?

issue_body:
With 15387dc3a8 the conform code now can handle the Japanese CSV sources like jp-nara.json. `process_one` fails when trying to write `sample.json`:
    UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf8' codec can't decode byte 0x93 in position 0: invalid start byte
My guess is what's going on is the `excerpt()` function uses OGR to read all sources, but the OGR code can only read UTF-8. The Japanese CSV inputs are in a Shift-JIS variant.

@migurski do you have time to look at this or should I? I suspect the sampler will need to read a bunch of the CSV processing tags in order to sample correctly as a dictionary. Maybe it'd be easier just to publish the first 10 lines of the CSV file as a CSV sample. The conform code has a lot of CSV reading code, see `csv_source_to_csv()`. Unfortunately it also does some processing on the CSV that's not appropriate for sampling.

issue_title: AttributeError: module 'urllib' has no attribute 'urlencode' (Python 3, repository_blob)
issue_body:
Getting an issue in python 3.6 with the `project.repository_blob` function.
```
File "[blah blah blah]/lib/python3.6/site-packages/gitlab/v3/objects.py", line 1885, in repository_blob
    url += '?%s' % (urllib.urlencode({'filepath': filepath}))
AttributeError: module 'urllib' has no attribute 'urlencode'
```

urlencode was moved in py3 to `urllib.parse.urlencode` (https://docs.python.org/3/library/urllib.parse.html#urllib.parse.urlencode). For maintaining compatibility across python 2 and 3, consider six's [six.moves.urllib](https://pythonhosted.org/six/#module-six.moves) instead of urllib itself.

Tapioca should be able to recognise 3xx, 4xx, and 5xx HTTP responses and raise appropriate exceptions according to the problem.

Each service should be able to customise this exceptions to add more info about the error returned. This is important because each service has it's own way o providing human readable error information.

issue_title: ObjectType as data container with resolver
issue_body:
The [docs](http://docs.graphene-python.org/en/latest/types/objecttypes/#instances-as-data-containers) mention that `ObjectType`s can be used as data containers like so:

```python
peter = Person(first_name='Peter', last_name='Griffin')

peter.first_name # prints "Peter"
peter.last_name # prints "Griffin"
```

**But what if I want to get an attribute after it's passed through it's resolver?** Here's an example similar to my use case:

```python
class Person(ObjectType):
  first_name = String()
  last_name = String()
  height = Int()

  def resolve_height(self, info):
    return int(self.height)

person_data = {
  'first_name': 'Peter',
  'last_name': 'Griffin',
  'height': '70' # note that this is a string instead of an int
}

peter = Person(**person_data)

type(peter.height) # <type 'str'> - but I want this to be <type 'int'>
```

The reason I'm doing this is because I'm building a GraphQL wrapper around a REST API using graphene and I'm populating a class like this with data from the API, but there are cases where I want to expose a different type than the API does (like changing `height` from a string to an integer).

issue_title: Mutations now have no access to context, resolve methods broken
issue_body:
The context story seems very confused in the newest version. My understanding from [the graphql-js 0.5.0 release notes](https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/releases/tag/v0.5.0) is that all resolving functions should now have the signature `([self,] args, context, info)`, as opposed to `([self,] args, info)`.

At the moment, if I update a `resolve_NAME` method to take context, then it throws argument errors.

Also, mutations don't take context and therefore they cannot access context data. It think a patch similar to [this one](https://github.com/graphql/graphql-relay-js/pull/77) on graphql-relay-js is necessary for the relay mutations at least, but probably for all of them.

issue_title: No support for deferred model proxies
issue_body:
Due to an extremely complex operation, i need to use a RawQuerySet, I am able to get past some lack of support for using raw query sets, however I haven't found a way to handle the type mismatch with a deferred object (this also would occur from a queryset that uses .only(),

I get the error message Expected value of type "MyModelNode" but got: MyModel_Deferred__.....

Is there any way for me to modify MyModelNode to accept defereed model instances?

It seems like UUID model fields get converted here:
 https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-django/blob/master/graphene_django/converter.py#L78-L81

which means they show up as base64 encoded `SWRlbnRpdHlOb2RlOmMxYmZiNTFjLWUyYTItNGM3YS1hOWQ5LTNiMDA4ODA2YmFhOA==` in query responses. However, when sending them back in a mutation, it complains `badly formed hexadecimal UUID string` in the error field. Sending the hex instead (e.g. `c1bfb51c-e2a2-4c7a-a9d9-3b008806baa8` works but is annoying to not be able to turn it around.

At the moment I'm using a helper function to decode and strip the `IdentityNode:` from the front:
```
def uuid_hex_from_b64(encoded):
    decoded = base64.b64decode(encoded)
    return decoded.split(b":")[1]
```

and use it like: 
```
new_club = {
    "name": input.get('name'),
    "treasurer_id": uuid_hex_from_b64(input.get('treasurer_id'))
}
club = Club.objects.create(**new_club)
```

This works but I'm not sure if this is a terrible idea or if it's sane, but some documentation of its usage as ok would save others like me hours of poking around! Thanks!

issue_title: Current metric name structure is hard to parse
issue_body:
Current metric name structure is hard to parse for other applications like influxdb(see more explanations [here](https://github.com/influxdb/influxdb/issues/2102#issuecomment-101566403))
So I propose the metric name to be in a more uniformed structure, as `<prefix><hostname>[suffix]<metric name><measure>[device]`, for example, `servers.localhost.cpu.irq.cpu0`, `servers.desolation.loadavg.01` and `servers.desolation.iostat.reads.sda`(Originally they are `servers.localhost.cpu.cpu0.irq`, `servers.desolation.loadavg.01` and `servers.desolation.iostat.sda.reads`)
To do this, we need to patch some of the collectors. And I don't think these changes would break the use cases before.
Would it be possible to cut a release to update the PyPi package? did a pip install diamond yesterday and it installed the BrightCove version.

When using the ElbCollector in conjunction with the StatsdHandler a failure occurs that causes StatsdHandler to stop emitting metrics for all collectors. This message is logged:

> [2015-09-23 15:00:57,384] [MainThread] Traceback (most recent call last):
>  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/diamond/handler/Handler.py", line 98, in _flush
>    self.flush()
>  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/diamond/handler/stats_d.py", line 142, in flush
>    self._send()
>  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/diamond/handler/stats_d.py", line 129, in _send
>    value = value - self.old_values[metric.path]
> TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for -: 'NoneType' and 'NoneType'

Upon inspection of stats_d.py, it attempts to calculate a delta for non-gauge values so that it can send a counter (lines 125-136). The relative code is lines 127-130:

``` python
value = metric.raw_value
if metric.path in self.old_values:
    value = value - self.old_values[metric.path]
self.old_values[metric.path] = metric.raw_value
```

The TypeError we see in the log is related to the - operand on types None and None (line 129).

We know, based on this error, that metric.raw_value is NoneType, so I tracked down where that originates from within ElbCollector. Here is the method signature for publish_delayed_metric() (lines 183-206):

``` python
def publish_delayed_metric(self, name, value, timestamp, raw_value=None,
                           precision=0, metric_type='GAUGE',
                           instance=None):
```

None of the places where publish_delayed_metric is called in elb.py set raw_value to anything explicitly, so it is always going to be NoneType. This would not be a problem but some of the metrics sent by ElbCollector are of type COUNTER.

I would like to fix this and enter a pull request, but wanted some guidance as to the coding standards of the project. What is the difference between raw_value and value? If we have ElbCollector always set raw_value equal to value, would this fix the problem?

Thanks.

issue_title: When tagging a release, also make a PyPi release
issue_body: We should also make a PyPi release whenever a release is being tagged.

There are six downloads related to 1.4 on [PyPI](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/robotframework-selenium2library/1.4.0). For a casual user knowing what to use is pretty hard, especially when the installation instructions only say `pip install ...`. In the future I would be ready to have only the `.tar.gz`. This would have various benefits:
- Easier to pick the right package.
- Easier to document the installation procedure (see also #255).
- Less work creating and uploading packages (running `python setup.py sdist register upload` is enough).

The reason I think we are fine with `.tar.gz` is that it is enough both for pip and for manual installation. Why the other packages aren't really giving anything but extra work is explained below:
- `.win32.exe` and `.win-amd64.exe`: These aren't enough to actually install the library. Some of the dependencies need to be installed using source distributions anyway, so it would probably be easier to just document the manual installation procedure to always use them (#255). Creating these packages also requires access to two separate Windows machines.
- `.zip`: Current Windows machines should be able to extract `.tar.gz` just fine so having two source distributions doesn't give much. Creating this package is possible also outside Windows so it's not too big a task.
- `-py2.6.egg` and `-py2.7.egg`: I don't know any use case for these.

If any of the above packages have valid use cases they obviously should be kept, but adding a note about the usage to installation instructions might be a good idea then. If all of the packages are valid, this issue should be closed as invalid instead.

Originally on 2014-03-11

A record can have several dates:
* Record creation (from `bibrec` table
* Publication date (`260__c`, `269__c`)
* Journal year (`773__y`)
* In general `260__c`, `269__c`, `111__x`, `502__d`, `773__y`

For sorting purposes (e.g. in in BibSort) or for plotting citation graph, it is needed to retrieve the earliest date that could represent when a document was first known.

For this reason a new config variable is advisable such as:

```python

CFG_BIBRANK_RECORD_EARLIEST_DATES = [
   ('260__c', ['%Y-%m-%d', '%Y']),
   ...
]
```

with the proposed syntax and interpretation: every element of the list represent a date that can be potential retrieved. Every element is a tuple, where the first element is a MARC tag, and the second is a list of format-dates that are going to be applied in order to parse the value of the date.

A new function will then be created:

```python
def get_record_earliest_date(recid, record=None):
    pass
```

Which given a `recid` (and an optional pre-parsed `record` in `recstruct` format, for performance needs), will return the earliest possible date extracting it from the record using the before describe parameters plus the record creation_date timestamp. In case a date resolves to a partial date (say only to a year or to a month), then it is completed with January and the 1st of the month (as necessary). If however for the same year(-month) an other date is available and is more precise, than this latest one is considered.

So say:

```
2013-05-03
2013
```

dates are available. Then the the date: `2013-05-03` will be returned

If:

```
2013-05-03
2012
```

dates are available, then the date: `2012-01-01` will be returned.

It would be great to introduce an audit log for all the modifications that are performed online to knowledge bases (sort of who added/updated/deleted what and when), so that it's possible to check the history of modification and also undo changes in case of mistakes.

issue_title: RFC formatter: support only jinja formatting + heavy API refactoring (principles)
issue_body:
While this is not the most urgent issue, I'd like a general opinion on if this is the way to go in the future. The RFC would have to be fleshed out to be more specific, so this is just the about agreeing on some principles.

**Problem:**
Currently we support many different formatting engines with lots of legacy code which makes maintenance hard. The current code is mixing responsibilities - e.g. formatting of a record representation, fetching of that record, caching, debug, which also makes maintenance and replacement of individual parts hard. New code written for overlays is in danger of depending too much on legacy code (using e..g existing bfe_elements), thus making transition harder in the future. 

**Proposal:**

- [x] Support only Jinja based formatting. Meaning bfe elements, XSLT transformation (which anyway requires MARCXML) and bfx will disappear.
- [x] Rebuild module from scratch with a clean API. I.e. de-scope module to only deal with formatting of a record representation (e.g. ``format_record`` should not take a recid, but say a JSON representation instead). Create separate modules to deal with caching, etc.
- [x] Decide on responsibilities between records and formatting module (i imagine records is incharge of transformation, while formatter takes a transformation and renders it).
- [x] Existing data model with format template and output formats will be kept.
- [ ] Build legacy layer that implements most important methods from old api, using the new modules. Likely perhaps even the old engines could be supported if specifically enabled. Legacy layer must be completely separate from new module.

P.S Don't shoot me :-)

**Issues raised in comments:**

* Dealing with dynamic data.
* Engines concept useful to abstract underlying technology.
* Transformation between data models (MARC->DC, DC->DataCite, ...)
* Need for high level API responsible for orchestration of small isolated modules.

- [ ] investigate what is the correct dependency order with *Invenio-Accounts*
- [ ] add missing dependencies

--
cc @hachreak @lnielsen

issue_title: global: load tasks via entry points
issue_body:
Add support for loading tasks via entry_points and modules.

Invenio modules should be using the [``@shared_task``](http://docs.celeryproject.org/en/latest/django/first-steps-with-django.html#using-the-shared-task-decorator) decorator to declare tasks.

The [celery application](https://github.com/lnielsen/mysite/blob/master/mysite/celery.py) needs a way to load these tasks. This should be possible via providing entry points and/or list of modules.

```python
# setup.py (declaring celery tasks in a Invenio module).
setup(
    entry_points={
        'invenio_celery.tasks': [
            'invenio_fungenerator = invenio_fungenerator',
        ]
    }
)
```

The celery application should then be able to load the tasks similar to this:
```python
from flask_celeryext import create_celery_app
from invenio_celery import load_tasks
from .factory import create_app
celery = create_celery_app(create_app)
celery.autodiscover_tasks(load_tasks(entry_point='invenio_celery.tasks'))
```

For instance on ``GET /api/files/:bucket``
- ``delimiter``
- ``max-keys``
- ``prefix``
- ``start-after``

In Zenodo we currently have a custom CLI implemented, specific to our usecases for file replacement.

``invenio files list [OPTIONS]``
``invenio files add [file] [OPTIONS]``
``invenio files remove [key] [OPTIONS]``
``invenio files rename [old_key] [new_key] [OPTIONS]``

In the end the commands needs to determine a bucket on which the operations are to be made, one obvious choice for [OPTIONS] which would specify that would be:
``--bucket/-b <bucket UUID>``
however, one would also want to have a direct replacement on a record or deposit, e.g.:
``--recid/-r <recid>`` or at least a pair ``--type/-t <pid_type> --value/-v <pid_value>``, so that the following is also possible:

``invenio files add ./file.txt -t recid -v 12345``

issue_title: AbsoluteURLFor: Field parameters are passed to URL
issue_body:
I use `AbsoluteURLFor` for generating an URL to an image:

```python
from flask_marshmallow import Marshmallow
from flask_marshmallow.fields import AbsoluteURLFor
from marshmallow import fields

ma = Marshmallow()

class TestSchema(ma.Schema):
    my_logo = AbsoluteURLFor(
        'logo_api',  # /api/logos/<id>
        id='<logo_id>',
        dump_to='myLogo'
    )
```

The resulting response is:

```json
{
  "myLogo": "http://localhost:5000/api/logos/1?dump_to=myLogo"
}
```

The expected result is:

```json
{
  "myLogo": "http://localhost:5000/api/logos/1"
}
```

If I skip the `dump_to`-parameter the result is:

```json
{
  "my_logo": "http://localhost:5000/api/logos/1"
}
```

This is because the field parameter `dump_to` is passed as a parameter to `flask.url_for`. But this is (of course) not my intention. Can this be fixed or do I have to use another field like `Function` for example?

samfile.pileup("reference_chr",10,20)

It would appear that the attribute `AlignmentFile.is_bam` is only reflecting wether the `b` flag used to open a given SAM file, instead of testing the content of the file and ascertaining if it is actually a BAM.  As an example:

```
>>> sam = pysam.AlignmentFile("example.sam", 'rb')
>>> sam.is_bam
True
>>> sam = pysam.AlignmentFile("example.sam", 'r')
>>> sam.is_bam
False
```

Since BAM files are built with a standard [BZGF header](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gzip#File_format), would it not make sense to test the file for the GZIP magic string `0x1F8B` at the start of the file?

issue_title: Why refer to case from institute?
issue_body:
Whe should not need this, the case in itself have all information that is necessary.
So when removing a case we do not need to update institutes, seems like a more mongodb way of doing it.

Today I heard a talk by Gregory Snyder ( @gsnyder206 ) in which he showed something super cool:
He's been making synthetic galaxy images from the Illustris simulation to simulate data in various passbands.  Examples are
http://www.stsci.edu/~gsnyder/hudftool.html

They are working to put these into GalaxyZoo to get morphology classifications, too.  So this immediately made me think it would be very interesting to have these in GalSim (apparently @msimet had the same idea when she heard this talk too).  The basic idea is:
- Okay, they aren't real galaxies.  But they are realistically complicated, which is very interesting for studies of model bias and so on.
- Unlike our current RealGalaxy catalog from COSMOS, they are chromatic.
- Even when we have chromatic RealGalaxy catalogs from AEGIS, those will be based on just two bands and thus involve a bunch of assumptions.  So in some sense these fully chromatic ones from Illustris may be "better" (though within the limitations imposed by hydro simulations not making perfectly realistic galaxies).

A few questions:
1) How should we think about the resolution limit of the simulations?  Should we be treating that like a PSF of sorts, perhaps a Gaussian of some width?  If so, then GalSim can easily be made to take that into account when matching to the target PSF.

2) How is the information from the simulations available?  In particular, how is the color and redshift information encoded?  GalSim knows how to take images of the surface brightness profile, but for each galaxy there are presumably two other dimensions (wavelength, redshift) and I would like to understand how those are encoded in order to figure out how to get GalSim to use that information.

Anybody have any thoughts about this idea?  Agree/disagree about it being worthwhile?  @rmjarvis , @jmeyers314 , ...?  I am excited about this and could put some time into it over the summer, though I won't complain about sharing the load if there are any volunteers who want to be involved.

The pubsub library should support detecting and using the Pub/Sub emulator
issue_body:
The Pub/Sub emulator can be run, and the `PUBSUB_HOSTPORT` environment variable exported, with:

```
gcloud beta emulators pubsub start &
$(gcloud beta emulators pubsub env-init)
```

The pubsub libraries should detect this environment variable and connect to the emulator when it is available, similar to how it works for the Datastore emulator.

gRPC connections currently don't have enough information in the request for us to do efficient traffic management/routing. 

Will you add a header that allows for us to optimize for this on the server? The header format is:

```
name: google-cloud-resource-prefix
value: projects/my-project-id
```

In general, the prefix would be the same as the JSON-over-http URL up to any custom method. For example, a Cloud Datastore commit message over JSON would hit the url `projects/my-project-id:commit`.

Here is an example usage in the cloud-bigtable-client: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/cloud-bigtable-client/pull/915/files#diff-1078787e69bc1d7c5bb8507f0a87a143R83

issue_title: Pub/sub: amount of streaming pull operations way higher than streaming ack operations
issue_body:
We are using `python 2.7.12` with `google-cloud-pubsub==0.30.1` and `grpcio==1.9.1` and have a very basic subscriber script:

    import random
    import time

    from google.cloud import pubsub

    GCLOUD_PROJECT_ID = <our_project_id>
    SUBSCRIPTION_NAME = <subscription_name>

    def pubsub_message_callback(message):
        time.sleep(random.random())
        message.ack()

    def main():
        sub_client = pubsub.SubscriberClient()
        full_subscription_name = 'projects/{project_id}/subscriptions/{subscription}'.format(
            project_id=GCLOUD_PROJECT_ID,
            subscription=SUBSCRIPTION_NAME,
        )
        pubsub_subscription = sub_client.subscribe(
            full_subscription_name,
            flow_control=pubsub.types.FlowControl(max_messages=1)
        )
        pubsub_subscription.open(
            pubsub_message_callback
        )
        while True:
            pass

    if __name__ == '__main__':
        main()

When having a large backlog and running this script for 20 minutes, the amount of streaming pull operations is 10 times the amount of streaming ack operations. [(see image)](https://i.stack.imgur.com/rFa7l.png)

As the the flow control is set to `max_messages=1` I would expect that it will always wait to ack the received message, before receiving another one. And even when flow control was not set to `max_messages=1` it still looks like unintended behaviour as the total amount of pull operations is still way higher than the amount of ack operations (so what happens with those received/unacked messages?). Is this a bug or is it intended behaviour?

Also asked this as a question on [stackoverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/48640525/gcp-pub-sub-python-client-amount-of-streaming-pull-messages-way-higher-than-str)

Reference: https://github.com/daspecster/google-cloud-python/blob/add-storage-snippets/docs/storage_snippets.py

A few questions I have...
1. Should I make a PR to merge in at this point since we're not currently running snippets AFAICT?
2. If we're running these as system tests(which makes sense to me), I'm not sure how to handle things like email addresses for users and other account information([example](https://github.com/daspecster/google-cloud-python/blob/42e40cfc5b1bc8b91ecdcf603ae16485f09618ab/docs/storage_snippets.py#L71)).
3. If I should make mocks instead of hitting the service, it seems like that defeats the purpose unless I can inherit from the real class and just override the methods or some similar strategy?

Prevent CMD windows being shown when starting git in a subprocess.

Each repo that is opened creates a git cat-file processs and that provess will create

[patch.txt](https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/files/390557/patch.txt)

I was thankful to see your reaction to my issue #453, but unfortunately GitPython 2.0.9 has some issues on Python 2.6. GitPython 2.0.8 worked like a charm for us.

The issues are:
- uses of dictionary comprehension in `git/cmd.py` around line 800 (dict comprehensions were introduced in py27).
- use of SkipTest and skipIf from `unittest.case` in `git/util.py` and in several test cases (these were added to `unittest` in py27). Also, the Python `unittest` docs suggest they be imported from `unittest` and not from `unittest.case`, but that is minor.

I have fixed those locally and will create a PR for your review.

issue_title: Hide layers in order to avoid performance issues
issue_body:
#### Please add a code sample or a nbviewer link, copy-pastable if possible

```python
m = folium.Map(location=[48.9, 2.3], tiles="stamentoner", zoom_start=10)

for hour in st_dbscan_data:
    fg = folium.FeatureGroup(name=hour, overlay=True)
    current_clusters = st_dbscan_data[hour]['clusters']
    for index, cluster in enumerate(current_clusters):
        folium.plugins.MarkerCluster(
            locations=cluster,
            name='cluster %d' % index
        ).add_to(fg)
    fg.add_to(m)
    
folium.LayerControl().add_to(m)
    
m
```
#### Problem description

I am using `Jupyter` and `folium` to render and visualize clusters of points in a map.

Currently, I want to visualize my points, grouped in clusters, per hour (between 6AM and 10PM).
Each hour contains ~ 4 clusters (`cluster` in code), and the number of points per hour is ~ 500 points (so, 125 points per cluster).

I wanted to create a `MarkerCluster` per cluster of points, and a `FeatureGroup` per hour, in order to visualize the clusters hour after hour...

The problem here is that the rendering is very slow, and Jupyter does not display the map after the rendering (I checked the source code in my browser console, and the body of the cell is null - no error in the Jupyter/browser console either).

I think this behaviour is the result of performance issues, so I wanted to know if an option exists to disable the rendering of each `FeatureGroup` when building the map ?
In this case, I would like to enable the rendering "on the fly" when checking the layer in the map, and avoid the performance issues.
I checked the documentation for both `FeatureGroup` and `MarkerCluster`, but I did not found anything useful...

Thanks a lot :)
I'm using folium here, and I realized that the master version has https://github.com/python-visualization/folium/pull/723 already, but I could not install if because it's not in pip yet.
There is a missing condition in NearMiss implying that a warning is always raised.

issue_title: SpeechToTextV1.add_corpus() uses wrong http method (GET, should use POST)
issue_body:
Currently, using `add_corpus` will break with a `WatsonException: Unknown error` error. This is due to the method using the wrong HTTP method (GET, instead of POST). The tests don't catch this because the assertion in `test_custom_corpora` only checks the number of requests made and not the details of those requests.

I have a patch that should be ready soon.
"segmenttation_series" should be "segmentation_series"

## 2) Feature Request

### Problem/Use Case

We currently use a locally modified of `python-versioneer`. See https://github.com/NeurodataWithoutBorders/pynwb/commit/03408642ee51c47e2302bbd76d01b477f0b9b9f2

```
[This commit] customizes versionner so that it supports "tag_prefix"
specified as glob pattern.

Doing so allows to specify a glob pattern like "*.*.*" ensuring that
only tag matching our convention are considered.

issue_title: support secondaryFiles for File in CommandInputRecordField
issue_body:
I have a input parameter that is an array of records, like the following:

    resources:
      type:
        type: array
        items:
        - type: record
          name: resource
          fields:
            - name: resource
              type: File
              inputBinding:
                prefix: --resource
            - name: resource_param
              type: string
              inputBinding:
                prefix: --resource_param

In the record, resource is File object, and will require `secondaryFiles`. But `secondaryFiles` is only allowed for `CommandInputParameter`, and not allowed in `CommandInputRecordField`. Ideally I want to something like the following to work:

    resources:
      type:
        type: array
        items:
        - type: record
          name: resource
          fields:
            - name: resource
              type: File
              secondaryFiles:
                - ".ext"
              inputBinding:
                prefix: --resource
            - name: resource_param
              type: string
              inputBinding:
                prefix: --resource_param
My reason to use array of records here is to combine resource and resource_param together to produce command line like `--resource --resource_param --resource --resource_param`. 
One fix is to support `secondaryFiles` for File in CommandInputRecordField:
    "In `schemas/v1.0/Process.yml`, there is a `secondaryFiles` field under `Parameter`. Just copy this field to under InputRecordField after label field. "
This was posted as a question on BioStars ( https://www.biostars.org/p/263577/#263643 ).
